```svelte
<Stack gap={6}>
  <RadioButtonGroup
    legendText="Storage tier (disk)"
    name="plan"
    bind:selected={plan}
  >
    {#each plans as value (value)}
      <RadioButton labelText={value} {value} />
    {/each}
  </RadioButtonGroup>
  <Stack gap={4}>
    <div>
      {#each plans as value (value)}
        <Button
          size="small"
          kind="secondary"
          disabled={plan === value}
          on:click={() => (plan = value)}
        >
          Select "{value}"
        </Button>
      {/each}
    </div>
    <div>
      Selected plan:
      <strong>{plan}</strong>
    </div>
  </Stack>
</Stack>
```

### Left-aligned label text

Position labels to the left of the radio buttons.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RadioButton, RadioButtonGroup } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<RadioButtonGroup
  labelPosition="left"
  legendText="Storage tier (disk)"
  name="plan-left-aligned"
  selected="standard"
>
  <RadioButton labelText="Free (1 GB)" value="free" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Standard (10 GB)" value="standard" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Pro (128 GB)" value="pro" />
</RadioButtonGroup>
```

### Disabled buttons

Disable specific radio buttons to prevent selection.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RadioButton, RadioButtonGroup } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<RadioButtonGroup
  labelPosition="left"
  legendText="Storage tier (disk)"
  name="plan-disabled"
  selected="standard"
>
  <RadioButton disabled labelText="Free (1 GB)" value="free" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Standard (10 GB)" value="standard" />
  <RadioButton disabled labelText="Pro (128 GB)" value="pro" />
</RadioButtonGroup>
```

### Helper text

Use `helperText` to provide additional context or instructions below the radio
button group.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RadioButton, RadioButtonGroup } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<RadioButtonGroup
  legendText="Notification preferences"
  name="plan-helper"
  selected="email"
  helperText="You can change this setting at any time"
>
  <RadioButton labelText="Email" value="email" />
  <RadioButton labelText="SMS" value="sms" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Push notifications" value="push" />
</RadioButtonGroup>
```

### Vertical orientation

Display radio buttons in a vertical layout.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RadioButton, RadioButtonGroup } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<RadioButtonGroup
  orientation="vertical"
  legendText="Storage tier (disk)"
  name="plan-vertical"
  selected="standard"
>
  <RadioButton labelText="Free (1 GB)" value="free" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Standard (10 GB)" value="standard" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Pro (128 GB)" value="pro" />
</RadioButtonGroup>
```

### Skeleton

Show a loading state for horizontal radio buttons.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    RadioButtonGroup,
    RadioButtonSkeleton,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<RadioButtonGroup legendText="Storage tier (disk)">
  <RadioButtonSkeleton />
  <RadioButtonSkeleton />
  <RadioButtonSkeleton />
</RadioButtonGroup>
```

### Skeleton (vertical)

Show a loading state for vertical radio buttons.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    RadioButtonGroup,
    RadioButtonSkeleton,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<RadioButtonGroup orientation="vertical" legendText="Storage tier (disk)">
  <RadioButtonSkeleton />
  <RadioButtonSkeleton />
  <RadioButtonSkeleton />
</RadioButtonGroup>
```

---

### Component API

#### `RadioButtonGroup` props

| Prop                  | Type                         | Description                                                                       | Default        |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------- |
| `selected` (Reactive) | `Value \| undefined`         | Set the selected radio button value.                                              | _undefined_    |
| `disabled`            | `boolean`                    | Set to `true` to disable the radio buttons                                        | `false`        |
| `required`            | `boolean`                    | Set to `true` to require the selection of a radio button.                         | _undefined_    |
| `name`                | `string`                     | Specify a name attribute for the radio button inputs.                             | _undefined_    |
| `legendText`          | `string`                     | Specify the legend text.<br />Alternatively, use the named slot "legendChildren". | `""`           |
| `hideLegend`          | `boolean`                    | Set to `true` to visually hide the legend                                         | `false`        |
| `helperText`          | `string`                     | Specify the helper text                                                           | `""`           |
| `labelPosition`       | `"right" \| "left"`          | Specify the label position.                                                       | `"right"`      |
| `orientation`         | `"horizontal" \| "vertical"` | Specify the orientation of the radio buttons.                                     | `"horizontal"` |
| `id`                  | `string`                     | Set an id for the container div element.                                          | _undefined_    |

#### `RadioButtonGroup` slots

| Slot             | Detail                  |
| ---------------- | ----------------------- |
| `default`        | `Record<string, never>` |
| `legendChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `RadioButtonGroup` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `RadioButtonGroup` dispatched events

| Event       | Detail  |
| ----------- | ------- |
| `on:change` | `Value` |

#### `RadioButtonGroup` $$restProps

`RadioButtonGroup` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.

#### `RadioButton` props

| Prop                 | Type                       | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Default                             |
| -------------------- | -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `checked` (Reactive) | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to check the radio button                                                                                                                                                                           | `false`                             |
| `ref` (Reactive)     | `null \| HTMLInputElement` | Obtain a reference to the input HTML element                                                                                                                                                                      | `null`                              |
| `value`              | `Value`                    | Specify the value of the radio button.                                                                                                                                                                            | `""`                                |
| `disabled`           | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to disable the radio button                                                                                                                                                                         | `false`                             |
| `required`           | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to mark the field as required                                                                                                                                                                       | `false`                             |
| `labelPosition`      | `"right" \| "left"`        | Specify the label position.                                                                                                                                                                                       | `"right"`                           |
| `labelText`          | `string`                   | Specify the label text                                                                                                                                                                                            | `""`                                |
| `hideLabel`          | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to visually hide the label text                                                                                                                                                                     | `false`                             |
| `id`                 | `string`                   | Set an id for the input element                                                                                                                                                                                   | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `name`               | `string`                   | Specify a name attribute for the radio button input.<br />When multiple standalone RadioButton components share the same `name`,<br />they form an implicit group and their `checked` state will be synchronized. | _undefined_                         |

#### `RadioButton` slots

| Slot            | Detail                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------- |
| `labelChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `RadioButton` forwarded events

| Event       |
| ----------- |
| `on:blur`   |
| `on:change` |
| `on:focus`  |

#### `RadioButton` $$restProps

`RadioButton` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.

#### `RadioButtonSkeleton` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `RadioButtonSkeleton` $$restProps

`RadioButtonSkeleton` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.


## RadioTile

Radio tiles provide a selectable tile interface for choosing a single option
from a set. They support both individual and grouped usage, with options for
light and disabled states.

### Default

Create a group of radio tiles with a shared name and legend.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RadioTile, TileGroup } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TileGroup legendText="Service pricing tiers" name="plan" selected="standard">
  <RadioTile value="lite">Lite plan</RadioTile>
  <RadioTile value="standard">Standard plan</RadioTile>
  <RadioTile value="plus">Plus plan</RadioTile>
</TileGroup>
```

### Reactive (one-way binding)

Update the selected value using the `select` event.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RadioTile, TileGroup } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  const values = ["Lite plan", "Standard plan", "Plus plan"];

  let selected = values[1];
</script>

<TileGroup
  legendText="Service pricing tiers"
  name="plan"
  on:select={({ detail }) => (selected = detail)}
>
  {#each values as value}
    <RadioTile {value} checked={selected === value}>{value}</RadioTile>
  {/each}
</TileGroup>

<br />
Selected: <strong>{selected}</strong>
```

### Reactive (two-way binding)

Bind to the `selected` prop for simpler state management.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    RadioTile,
    Stack,
    TileGroup,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  const values = ["Lite plan", "Standard plan", "Plus plan"];

  let selected = values[1];
</script>

<Stack gap={5}>
  <TileGroup legendText="Service pricing tiers" name="plan" bind:selected>
    {#each values as value}
      <RadioTile {value}>{value}</RadioTile>
    {/each}
  </TileGroup>
  <div>Selected: <strong>{selected}</strong></div>
  <Button
    size="small"
    disabled={selected === values[1]}
    on:click={() => (selected = values[1])}
  >
    Set to "{values[1]}"
  </Button>
</Stack>
```

### Light variant

Use the light variant for light backgrounds.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RadioTile, TileGroup } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TileGroup
  legendText="Service pricing tiers"
  name="plan-light"
  selected="standard"
>
  <RadioTile light value="lite">Lite plan</RadioTile>
  <RadioTile light value="standard">Standard plan</RadioTile>
  <RadioTile light value="plus">Plus plan</RadioTile>
</TileGroup>
```

### Disabled state

Disable specific tiles to prevent selection.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RadioTile, TileGroup } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TileGroup
  legendText="Service pricing tiers"
  name="plan-disabled"
  selected="standard"
>
  <RadioTile value="lite" disabled>Lite plan</RadioTile>
  <RadioTile value="standard">Standard plan</RadioTile>
  <RadioTile value="plus" disabled>Plus plan</RadioTile>
</TileGroup>
```

---

### Component API

#### `TileGroup` props

| Prop                  | Type             | Description                                                                       | Default     |
| --------------------- | ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| `selected` (Reactive) | `T \| undefined` | Specify the selected tile value.                                                  | _undefined_ |
| `disabled`            | `boolean`        | Set to `true` to disable the tile group                                           | `false`     |
| `required`            | `boolean`        | Set to `true` to require the selection of a radio button.                         | _undefined_ |
| `name`                | `string`         | Specify a name attribute for the radio button inputs.                             | _undefined_ |
| `legendText`          | `string`         | Specify the legend text.<br />Alternatively, use the named slot "legendChildren". | `""`        |

#### `TileGroup` slots

| Slot             | Detail                  |
| ---------------- | ----------------------- |
| `default`        | `Record<string, never>` |
| `legendChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `TileGroup` dispatched events

| Event       | Detail |
| ----------- | ------ |
| `on:select` | `T`    |

#### `TileGroup` $$restProps

`TileGroup` spreads `$$restProps` to the `fieldset` element.

#### `RadioTile` props

| Prop                 | Type                            | Description                                              | Default                             |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `checked` (Reactive) | `boolean`                       | Set to `true` to check the tile                          | `false`                             |
| `light`              | `boolean`                       | Set to `true` to enable the light variant                | `false`                             |
| `disabled`           | `boolean`                       | Set to `true` to disable the tile                        | `false`                             |
| `required`           | `boolean`                       | Set to `true` to mark the field as required              | `false`                             |
| `value`              | `Value`                         | Specify the value of the radio input.                    | `""`                                |
| `tabindex`           | `number \| string \| undefined` | Specify the tabindex                                     | `"0"`                               |
| `iconDescription`    | `string`                        | Specify the ARIA label for the radio tile checkmark icon | `"Tile checkmark"`                  |
| `id`                 | `string`                        | Set an id for the input element                          | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `name`               | `string`                        | Specify a name attribute for the radio tile input.       | _undefined_                         |

#### `RadioTile` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `RadioTile` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:change`     |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:keydown`    |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `RadioTile` $$restProps

`RadioTile` spreads `$$restProps` to the `label` element.


## RecursiveList

Recursive lists provide a flexible way to render hierarchical data structures
using either unordered or ordered lists. They support nested nodes, links, and
HTML content, making them ideal for displaying complex data hierarchies.

> [!WARNING] HTML content provided via the `html` prop is not sanitized.

### Default

Create a hierarchical list using the `nodes` prop. Each node can contain text,
links, HTML content, and nested nodes.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RecursiveList } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  const nodes = [
    {
      text: "Item 1",
      nodes: [
        {
          text: "Item 1a",
          nodes: [{ html: "<h5>HTML content</h5>" }],
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      text: "Item 2",
      nodes: [
        {
          href: "https://svelte.dev/",
        },
        {
          href: "https://svelte.dev/",
          text: "Link with custom text",
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      text: "Item 3",
    },
  ];
</script>

<RecursiveList {nodes} />
```

### Ordered

Set `type` to `"ordered"` to use numbered lists with proper indentation.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RecursiveList } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  const nodes = [
    {
      text: "Item 1",
      nodes: [
        {
          text: "Item 1a",
          nodes: [{ html: "<h5>HTML content</h5>" }],
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      text: "Item 2",
      nodes: [
        {
          href: "https://svelte.dev/",
        },
        {
          href: "https://svelte.dev/",
          text: "Link with custom text",
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      text: "Item 3",
    },
  ];
</script>

<RecursiveList type="ordered" {nodes} />
```

### Ordered (native styles)

Set `type` to `"ordered-native"` to use browser-native numbering styles.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RecursiveList } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  const nodes = [
    {
      text: "Item 1",
      nodes: [
        {
          text: "Item 1a",
          nodes: [{ html: "<h5>HTML content</h5>" }],
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      text: "Item 2",
      nodes: [
        {
          href: "https://svelte.dev/",
        },
        {
          href: "https://svelte.dev/",
          text: "Link with custom text",
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      text: "Item 3",
    },
  ];
</script>

<RecursiveList type="ordered-native" {nodes} />
```

### Flat data structure

Convert flat data structures to hierarchical arrays using the `toHierarchy`
utility function.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RecursiveList, toHierarchy } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  const nodesFlat = [
    { id: 1, text: "Item 1" },
    { id: 2, text: "Item 1a", pid: 1 },
    { id: 3, html: "<h5>HTML content</h5>", pid: 2 },
    { id: 4, text: "Item 2" },
    { id: 5, href: "https://svelte.dev/", pid: 4 },
    {
      id: 6,
      href: "https://svelte.dev/",
      text: "Link with custom text",
      pid: 4,
    },
    { id: 7, text: "Item 3" },
  ];
</script>

<RecursiveList nodes={toHierarchy(nodesFlat, (node) => node.pid)} />
```

---

### Component API

#### `RecursiveList` props

| Prop    | Type                                           | Description                         | Default       |
| ------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------- |
| `nodes` | `ReadonlyArray<Node & { nodes?: Node[] }>`     | Specify the nodes to render.        | `[]`          |
| `type`  | `"unordered" \| "ordered" \| "ordered-native"` | Specify the type of list to render. | `"unordered"` |

#### `RecursiveList` typedefs

```ts
type RecursiveListNode = {
  /** Node text content */
  text?: string;
  /** Node link URL */
  href?: string;
  /** Node HTML content */
  html?: string;
  /** Child nodes */
  nodes?: RecursiveListNode[];
};
```

#### `RecursiveList` $$restProps

`RecursiveList` spreads `$$restProps` to the `ul | ol` element.


## Search

Search provides a flexible search input with support for expandable variants,
different sizes, and custom icons. It includes built-in functionality for
clearing input and handling keyboard events.

### Default

The search input is extra-large by default. Use the `size` prop to choose
between [large](#large-size) and [small](#small-size) variants.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search />
```

### Default value

Set an initial value using the `value` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search placeholder="Search catalog..." value="Cloud functions" />
```

### Reactive example

Bind to the `value` prop for reactive updates.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, ButtonSet, Search, Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let value = "";
</script>

<Stack gap={5}>
  <Search bind:value />
  <div>
    <ButtonSet>
      <Button
        size="small"
        disabled={value === "Cloud functions"}
        on:click={() => (value = "Cloud functions")}
      >
        Set value
      </Button>
      <Button
        kind="ghost"
        size="small"
        disabled={value.length === 0}
        on:click={() => (value = "")}
      >
        Clear value
      </Button>
    </ButtonSet>
  </div>
  <div>Value: {value}</div>
</Stack>
```

### Clear event

The `clear` event is dispatched when clicking the clear button or pressing
<kbd>Escape</kbd>.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search value="Cloud functions" on:clear={() => console.log("clear")} />

## Expandable variant Enable the expandable variant to show a compact search
icon that expands on focus.
```

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let expanded = false;
</script>

<Search expandable bind:expanded on:expand on:collapse />

<br />

<strong>Expanded:</strong>
{expanded}
```

### Light variant

Use the light variant for light backgrounds.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search light />
```

### Large size

Set `size` to `"lg"` for a large search input.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search size="lg" />
```

### Small size

Set `size` to `"sm"` for a small search input.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search size="sm" />
```

### Disabled state

Disable the search input using the `disabled` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search disabled />
```

### Custom icon

Replace the default search icon with a custom one.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Query from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Query.svelte";
</script>

<Search icon={Query} />
```

### Skeleton

Display a loading state using the skeleton variant.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search skeleton />
```

### Skeleton (large)

Display a loading state for the large search variant.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search size="lg" skeleton />
```

### Skeleton (small)

Display a loading state for the small search variant.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Search } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Search size="sm" skeleton />
```

---

### Component API

#### `Search` props

| Prop                   | Type                       | Description                                             | Default                             |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `value` (Reactive)     | `T`                        | Specify the value of the search input.                  | `""`                                |
| `expanded` (Reactive)  | `boolean`                  | Set to `true to expand the search input                 | `false`                             |
| `ref` (Reactive)       | `null \| HTMLInputElement` | Obtain a reference to the input HTML element            | `null`                              |
| `size`                 | `"sm" \| "lg" \| "xl"`     | Specify the size of the search input.                   | `"xl"`                              |
| `searchClass`          | `string`                   | Specify the class name passed to the outer div element  | `""`                                |
| `skeleton`             | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to display the skeleton state             | `false`                             |
| `light`                | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to enable the light variant               | `false`                             |
| `disabled`             | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to disable the search input               | `false`                             |
| `expandable`           | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to enable the expandable variant          | `false`                             |
| `placeholder`          | `string`                   | Specify the `placeholder` attribute of the search input | `"Search..."`                       |
| `autocomplete`         | `"on" \| "off"`            | Specify the `autocomplete` attribute.                   | `"off"`                             |
| `autofocus`            | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to auto focus the search element          | `false`                             |
| `closeButtonLabelText` | `string`                   | Specify the close button label text                     | `"Clear search input"`              |
| `labelText`            | `string`                   | Specify the label text                                  | `""`                                |
| `icon`                 | `Icon`                     | Specify the icon to render.                             | `IconSearch`                        |
| `id`                   | `string`                   | Set an id for the input element                         | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |

#### `Search` slots

| Slot            | Detail                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------- |
| `labelChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `Search` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:blur`       |
| `on:change`     |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:focus`      |
| `on:input`      |
| `on:keydown`    |
| `on:keyup`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |
| `on:paste`      |

#### `Search` dispatched events

| Event         | Detail |
| ------------- | ------ |
| `on:clear`    | `null` |
| `on:collapse` | `null` |
| `on:expand`   | `null` |

#### `Search` $$restProps

`Search` spreads `$$restProps` to the `input` element.


## Select

Selects provide a dropdown menu for selecting a single option from a list. They
support various states, sizes, and include built-in validation and helper text.

### Default

Create a basic select menu with select item components. The first item's value
is used as the default if `selected` is not set.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Select, SelectItem } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Select
  labelText="Carbon theme"
  on:change={(e) => console.log("value", e.target.value)}
>
  <SelectItem value="white" />
  <SelectItem value="g10" />
  <SelectItem value="g80" />
  <SelectItem value="g90" />
  <SelectItem value="g100" />
</Select>
```

### Custom item text

Use the `text` prop to customize the display text of each option.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Select, SelectItem } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Select
  labelText="Carbon theme"
  on:change={(e) => console.log("value", e.target.value)}
>
  <SelectItem value="white" text="White" />
  <SelectItem value="g10" text="Gray 10" />
  <SelectItem value="g80" text="Gray 80" />
  <SelectItem value="g90" text="Gray 90" />
  <SelectItem value="g100" text="Gray 100" />
</Select>
```

### Initial selected value

Set the initial selection using the `selected` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Select, SelectItem } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Select
  labelText="Carbon theme"
  selected="g10"
  on:change={(e) => console.log("value", e.target.value)}
>
  <SelectItem value="white" text="White" />
  <SelectItem value="g10" text="Gray 10" />
  <SelectItem value="g80" text="Gray 80" />
  <SelectItem value="g90" text="Gray 90" />
  <SelectItem value="g100" text="Gray 100" />
</Select>
```

### Reactive example

The `selected` prop supports two-way binding for reactive updates.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, Select, SelectItem, Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let selected = "g10";
</script>

<Stack gap={6}>
  <Select
    labelText="Carbon theme"
    helperText="Selected: {selected}"
    bind:selected
  >
    <SelectItem value="white" text="White" />
    <SelectItem value="g10" text="Gray 10" />
    <SelectItem value="g80" text="Gray 80" />
    <SelectItem value="g90" text="Gray 90" />
    <SelectItem value="g100" text="Gray 100" />
  </Select>
  <Button
    kind="tertiary"
    size="small"
    disabled={selected === "g90"}
    on:click={() => (selected = "g90")}
  >
    Set to "g90"
  </Button>
</Stack>
```

### Helper text

Add descriptive text below the select menu.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Select, SelectItem } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Select
  helperText="Carbon offers 4 themes (2 light, 3 dark)"
  labelText="Carbon theme"
  selected="g10"
>
  <SelectItem value="white" text="White" />
  <SelectItem value="g10" text="Gray 10" />
  <SelectItem value="g80" text="Gray 80" />
  <SelectItem value="g90" text="Gray 90" />
  <SelectItem value="g100" text="Gray 100" />
</Select>
```

### Hidden label

Visually hide the label while maintaining accessibility.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Select, SelectItem } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Select hideLabel labelText="Carbon theme" selected="g10">
  <SelectItem value="white" text="White" />
  <SelectItem value="g10" text="Gray 10" />
  <SelectItem value="g80" text="Gray 80" />
  <SelectItem value="g90" text="Gray 90" />
  <SelectItem value="g100" text="Gray 100" />
</Select>
```

### Item groups

Group related options using select item group components.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Select,
    SelectItem,
    SelectItemGroup,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Select labelText="Carbon theme" selected="0">
  <SelectItem value="0" text="Select a theme" disabled hidden />
  <SelectItemGroup label="Light theme">
    <SelectItem value="white" text="White" />
    <SelectItem value="g10" text="Gray 10" />
  </SelectItemGroup>
  <SelectItemGroup label="Dark theme">
    <SelectItem value="g80" text="Gray 80" />
    <SelectItem value="g90" text="Gray 90" />
    <SelectItem value="g100" text="Gray 100" />
  </SelectItemGroup>
</Select>
```

### Component API

#### `Select` props

| Prop                  | Type                        | Description                                      | Default                             |
| --------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------- |
| `selected` (Reactive) | `Value \| undefined`        | Specify the selected item value.                 | _undefined_                         |
| `ref` (Reactive)      | `null \| HTMLSelectElement` | Obtain a reference to the select HTML element    | `null`                              |
| `size`                | `"sm" \| "xl"`              | Set the size of the select input.                | _undefined_                         |
| `inline`              | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to use the inline variant          | `false`                             |
| `light`               | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to enable the light variant        | `false`                             |
| `disabled`            | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to disable the select element      | `false`                             |
| `id`                  | `string`                    | Set an id for the select element                 | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `name`                | `string`                    | Specify a name attribute for the select element. | _undefined_                         |
| `invalid`             | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to indicate an invalid state       | `false`                             |
| `invalidText`         | `string`                    | Specify the invalid state text                   | `""`                                |
| `warn`                | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to indicate a warning state        | `false`                             |
| `warnText`            | `string`                    | Specify the warning state text                   | `""`                                |
| `helperText`          | `string`                    | Specify the helper text                          | `""`                                |
| `noLabel`             | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to not render a label              | `false`                             |
| `labelText`           | `string`                    | Specify the label text                           | `""`                                |
| `hideLabel`           | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to visually hide the label text    | `false`                             |
| `required`            | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to mark the field as required      | `false`                             |

#### `Select` slots

| Slot            | Detail                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------- |
| `default`       | `Record<string, never>` |
| `labelChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `Select` forwarded events

| Event       |
| ----------- |
| `on:blur`   |
| `on:change` |
| `on:focus`  |
| `on:input`  |

#### `Select` dispatched events

| Event       | Detail  | Description         |
| ----------- | ------- | ------------------- |
| `on:update` | `Value` | The selected value. |

#### `Select` $$restProps

`Select` spreads `$$restProps` to the `select` element.

#### `SelectItem` props

| Prop       | Type      | Description                                                                         | Default     |
| ---------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| `value`    | `Value`   | Specify the option value.                                                           | `""`        |
| `text`     | `string`  | Specify the option text.<br />If not specified, the value will be used as the text. | _undefined_ |
| `hidden`   | `boolean` | Set to `true` to hide the option                                                    | `false`     |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | Set to `true` to disable the option                                                 | `false`     |
| `class`    | `string`  | Specify the class of the `option` element.                                          | _undefined_ |
| `style`    | `string`  | Specify the style of the `option` element.                                          | _undefined_ |

#### `SelectItemGroup` props

| Prop       | Type      | Description                                         | Default           |
| ---------- | --------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | Set to `true` to disable the optgroup element       | `false`           |
| `label`    | `string`  | Specify the label attribute of the optgroup element | `"Provide label"` |

#### `SelectItemGroup` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `SelectItemGroup` $$restProps

`SelectItemGroup` spreads `$$restProps` to the `optgroup` element.

#### `SelectSkeleton` props

| Prop        | Type      | Description                          | Default |
| ----------- | --------- | ------------------------------------ | ------- |
| `hideLabel` | `boolean` | Set to `true` to hide the label text | `false` |

#### `SelectSkeleton` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `SelectSkeleton` $$restProps

`SelectSkeleton` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.


## SelectableTile

Selectable tiles provide a tile-based selection interface that allows users to
select multiple options. They support various states and include built-in
keyboard navigation and accessibility features.

### Multi-selectable tiles

Group multiple selectable tiles together for multi-selection scenarios.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    SelectableTile,
    SelectableTileGroup,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SelectableTileGroup
  legendText="Select options"
  name="options"
  selected={["option1", "option2"]}
>
  <SelectableTile value="option1">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
  <SelectableTile value="option2">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
  <SelectableTile value="option3">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
</SelectableTileGroup>
```

### Reactive (one-way binding)

Update the selected values using the `select` and `deselect` events.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    SelectableTile,
    SelectableTileGroup,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  const values = ["Lite plan", "Standard plan", "Plus plan"];

  let selected = [values[0], values[1]];
</script>

<SelectableTileGroup
  legendText="Service pricing tiers"
  name="plan"
  on:select={({ detail }) => (selected = [...selected, detail])}
  on:deselect={({ detail }) =>
    (selected = selected.filter((v) => v !== detail))}
>
  {#each values as value}
    <SelectableTile {value} selected={selected.includes(value)}>
      {value}
    </SelectableTile>
  {/each}
</SelectableTileGroup>

<br />
Selected: <strong>{selected.join(", ") || "None"}</strong>
```

### Reactive (two-way binding)

Bind to the `selected` prop for simpler state management.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    SelectableTile,
    SelectableTileGroup,
    Stack,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  const values = ["Lite plan", "Standard plan", "Plus plan"];

  let selected = [values[0], values[1]];
</script>

<Stack gap={5}>
  <SelectableTileGroup
    legendText="Service pricing tiers"
    name="plan"
    bind:selected
  >
    {#each values as value}
      <SelectableTile {value}>{value}</SelectableTile>
    {/each}
  </SelectableTileGroup>
  <div>Selected: <strong>{selected.join(", ") || "None"}</strong></div>
  <Button
    size="small"
    disabled={selected.length === 1 && selected[0] === values[1]}
    on:click={() => (selected = [values[1]])}
  >
    Set to "{values[1]}" only
  </Button>
</Stack>
```

### Light variant

Use the light variant for light backgrounds.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    SelectableTile,
    SelectableTileGroup,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SelectableTileGroup
  legendText="Select options"
  name="options-light"
  selected={["option1", "option2"]}
>
  <SelectableTile light value="option1">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
  <SelectableTile light value="option2">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
  <SelectableTile light value="option3">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
</SelectableTileGroup>
```

### Disabled state

Disable specific tiles to prevent interaction.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    SelectableTile,
    SelectableTileGroup,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SelectableTileGroup
  legendText="Select options"
  name="options-disabled"
  selected={["option1"]}
>
  <SelectableTile value="option1">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
  <SelectableTile disabled value="option2">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
  <SelectableTile disabled value="option3">Multi-select Tile</SelectableTile>
</SelectableTileGroup>
```

---

### Component API

#### `SelectableTileGroup` props

| Prop                  | Type                  | Description                                                                       | Default     |
| --------------------- | --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| `selected` (Reactive) | `T[]`                 | Specify the selected tile values.                                                 | `[]`        |
| `disabled`            | `boolean`             | Set to `true` to disable the tile group                                           | `false`     |
| `name`                | `string \| undefined` | Specify a name attribute for the checkbox inputs.                                 | _undefined_ |
| `legendText`          | `string`              | Specify the legend text.<br />Alternatively, use the named slot "legendChildren". | `""`        |

#### `SelectableTileGroup` slots

| Slot             | Detail                  |
| ---------------- | ----------------------- |
| `default`        | `Record<string, never>` |
| `legendChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `SelectableTileGroup` dispatched events

| Event         | Detail |
| ------------- | ------ |
| `on:deselect` | `T`    |
| `on:select`   | `T`    |

#### `SelectableTileGroup` $$restProps

`SelectableTileGroup` spreads `$$restProps` to the `fieldset` element.

#### `SelectableTile` props

| Prop                  | Type                            | Description                                                   | Default                             |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `selected` (Reactive) | `boolean`                       | Set to `true` to select the tile                              | `false`                             |
| `ref` (Reactive)      | `null \| HTMLInputElement`      | Obtain a reference to the input HTML element                  | `null`                              |
| `light`               | `boolean`                       | Set to `true` to enable the light variant                     | `false`                             |
| `disabled`            | `boolean`                       | Set to `true` to disable the tile                             | `false`                             |
| `title`               | `string`                        | Specify the title of the selectable tile                      | `"title"`                           |
| `value`               | `string`                        | Specify the value of the selectable tile                      | `"value"`                           |
| `tabindex`            | `number \| string \| undefined` | Specify the tabindex                                          | `"0"`                               |
| `iconDescription`     | `string`                        | Specify the ARIA label for the selectable tile checkmark icon | `"Tile checkmark"`                  |
| `id`                  | `string`                        | Set an id for the input element                               | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `name`                | `string`                        | Specify a name attribute for the input.                       | `""`                                |

#### `SelectableTile` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `SelectableTile` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:keydown`    |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `SelectableTile` dispatched events

| Event         | Detail   |
| ------------- | -------- |
| `on:deselect` | `string` |
| `on:select`   | `string` |

#### `SelectableTile` $$restProps

`SelectableTile` spreads `$$restProps` to the `label` element.


## SessionStorage

Session storage provides a reactive wrapper around the
[Window.sessionStorage API](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/sessionStorage).
Unlike [LocalStorage](/components/LocalStorage), data is cleared when the
browser tab is closed and is isolated per tab--each tab has its own storage. Use
session storage for temporary state that should not persist across sessions
(e.g., form drafts, wizard progress).

The component listens for the
[storage event](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/storage_event)
for consistency with the storage API.

### Basic example

See how the component maintains state within a session. Toggle the switch and
refresh the page to see the persisted state. Note that state is cleared when the
tab is closed.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SessionStorage, Toggle } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let toggled = false;
  let events = [];

  $: document.documentElement.setAttribute("theme", toggled ? "g100" : "white");
</script>

<SessionStorage
  key="dark-mode-session"
  bind:value={toggled}
  on:save={() => {
    events = [...events, { event: "on:save" }];
  }}
  on:update={({ detail }) => {
    events = [...events, { event: "on:update", detail }];
  }}
/>

<Toggle size="sm" labelText="Dark mode" bind:toggled />

<br />

<pre>{JSON.stringify(events, null, 2)}</pre>
```

### Reactive key

The `key` prop is also reactive, allowing you to dynamically switch between
different storage keys. This is useful for managing per-user settings or
multiple contexts.

In this example, each user has their own theme preference stored under a
separate key. When you switch users, the component automatically loads that
user's saved preference.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    RadioButton,
    RadioButtonGroup,
    SessionStorage,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let selectedUser = "user-1";
  let theme = "white";

  $: storageKey = `session-storage-reactive-example-${selectedUser}`;
</script>

<div style:margin-bottom="var(--cds-layout-01)">
  Try selecting different users and changing their theme preferences. Each
  user's preference is stored separately and persists within the session.
</div>

<SessionStorage key={storageKey} bind:value={theme} />

<RadioButtonGroup legendText="Select user" bind:selected={selectedUser}>
  <RadioButton labelText="User 1" value="user-1" />
  <RadioButton labelText="User 2" value="user-2" />
  <RadioButton labelText="User 3" value="user-3" />
</RadioButtonGroup>

<br />

<RadioButtonGroup legendText="Theme preference" bind:selected={theme}>
  <RadioButton labelText="White" value="white" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Gray 10" value="g10" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Gray 80" value="g80" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Gray 90" value="g90" />
  <RadioButton labelText="Gray 100" value="g100" />
</RadioButtonGroup>

<div
  style="margin-top: var(--cds-layout-01)"
  style:margin-bottom="var(--cds-layout-01)"
>
  <div>
    <strong>Current key:</strong>
    {storageKey}
  </div>
  <div>
    <strong>Current theme:</strong>
    {theme}
  </div>
</div>
```

### Clear item, clear all

The component provides methods to manage stored data:

| Method      | Description                       |
| ----------- | --------------------------------- |
| `clearItem` | Remove a specific key-value pair. |
| `clearAll`  | Remove all stored data.           |

Use `bind:this` to access these methods. In this example, try toggling the
switch, refreshing the page, then clearing the storage.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, SessionStorage, Toggle } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let storage;
  let toggled = false;

  $: document.documentElement.setAttribute("theme", toggled ? "g100" : "white");
</script>

<SessionStorage bind:this={storage} bind:value={toggled} />

<Toggle size="sm" labelText="Dark mode" bind:toggled />

<br />
<br />

<Button size="small" on:click={storage.clearAll}>Clear storage</Button>
```

---

### Component API

#### `SessionStorage` props

| Prop               | Type         | Description                                                       | Default                 |
| ------------------ | ------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- |
| `value` (Reactive) | `T`          | Provide a value to persist.                                       | `""`                    |
| `key`              | `string`     | Specify the session storage key.                                  | `"session-storage-key"` |
| `clearItem`        | `() => void` | Remove the persisted key value from the browser's session storage | _undefined_             |
| `clearAll`         | `() => void` | Clear all key values from the browser's session storage           | _undefined_             |

#### `SessionStorage` dispatched events

| Event       | Detail                                           | Description                                                                                                                       |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `on:save`   | `null`                                           |                                                                                                                                   |
| `on:update` | `{<br />  prevValue: T;<br />  value: T;<br />}` | Fires when the stored value changes, either from a bound value update or when sessionStorage is modified from another tab/window. |


## SkeletonIcon

`SkeletonIcon` wraps [SkeletonPlaceholder](/components/SkeletonPlaceholder) to
provide a loading-state placeholder for icon-sized areas. Carbon icons use a
16/20/24/32 pixel scale. However, you can use any number of pixels with the
`size` prop.

### Default (16px)

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonIcon />
```

### Size (20px)

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonIcon size={20} />
```

### Size (24px)

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonIcon size={24} />
```

### Size (32px)

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonIcon size={32} />
```

---

### Component API

#### `SkeletonIcon` props

| Prop   | Type                                      | Description                                                                                            | Default |
| ------ | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- |
| `size` | `(16 \| 20 \| 24 \| 32 \| (number & {}))` | Specify the icon size.<br />Carbon icons use a 16/20/24/32 scale, but any number (pixels) can be used. | `16`    |

#### `SkeletonIcon` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `SkeletonIcon` $$restProps

`SkeletonIcon` spreads `$$restProps` to the `SkeletonPlaceholder` component.


## SkeletonPlaceholder

Skeleton placeholders provide a loading state placeholder that can be used to
indicate content is being loaded. They support custom sizing and forward mouse
events for interactive loading states.

### Default

Create a basic skeleton placeholder with default styling.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonPlaceholder } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonPlaceholder />
```

### Custom size

Use the `size` prop to set both width and height.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonPlaceholder } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonPlaceholder size="8rem" />
```

### Custom size (number)

Number values default to pixels.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonPlaceholder } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonPlaceholder size={128} />
```

### Custom width and height

Use the `width` and `height` props for different dimensions.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonPlaceholder } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonPlaceholder width="20rem" height="10rem" />
```

### Custom width and height (number)

Number values default to pixels.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonPlaceholder } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonPlaceholder width={320} height={160} />
```

---

### Component API

#### `SkeletonPlaceholder` props

| Prop     | Type               | Description                                                                                           | Default     |
| -------- | ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| `size`   | `number \| string` | Shorthand size that sets both width and height.<br />Accepts a number (px) or string (e.g., "12rem"). | _undefined_ |
| `width`  | `number \| string` | Width of the placeholder.<br />Accepts a number (px) or string (e.g., "12rem").                       | _undefined_ |
| `height` | `number \| string` | Height of the placeholder.<br />Accepts a number (px) or string (e.g., "12rem").                      | _undefined_ |

#### `SkeletonPlaceholder` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `SkeletonPlaceholder` $$restProps

`SkeletonPlaceholder` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.


## SkeletonText

Skeleton text provides a loading state for text content with support for
different sizes and line counts. It's commonly used to indicate that text
content is being loaded.

### Default

Create a basic skeleton text with default styling.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonText } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonText />
```

### Heading variant

Use the heading variant for larger text placeholders.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonText } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonText heading />
```

### Paragraph variant

Use the paragraph variant for multi-line text placeholders.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonText } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonText paragraph />
```

### Paragraph with custom line count

Specify the number of lines to render using the `lines` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonText } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonText paragraph lines={8} />
```

### Paragraph with max width

Set a custom width for the text placeholder using the `width` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SkeletonText } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SkeletonText paragraph width="50%" />
```

---

### Component API

#### `SkeletonText` props

| Prop        | Type      | Description                                     | Default  |
| ----------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------- | -------- |
| `lines`     | `number`  | Specify the number of lines to render           | `3`      |
| `heading`   | `boolean` | Set to `true` to use the heading size variant   | `false`  |
| `paragraph` | `boolean` | Set to `true` to use the paragraph size variant | `false`  |
| `width`     | `string`  | Specify the width of the text (% or px)         | `"100%"` |

#### `SkeletonText` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `SkeletonText` $$restProps

`SkeletonText` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.


## Slider

Sliders provide a visual way to select a value from a range. They support
keyboard navigation, custom ranges, step values, and various states.

### Default

Create a basic slider with a label and default range (0-100).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider labelText="Instances" value={0} />
```

### Full width

Set `fullWidth` to `true` for the slider to span the full width of its
containing element.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider labelText="Instances" fullWidth value={0} />
```

### Hidden label

Visually hide the label while maintaining accessibility.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider labelText="Instances" hideLabel value={0} />
```

### Hidden text input

Hide the text input while keeping the slider functionality.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider labelText="Instances" hideTextInput value={0} />
```

### Custom minimum, maximum values

Set custom range values and labels.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider
  labelText="Runtime memory (MB)"
  min={10}
  max={990}
  maxLabel="990 MB"
  value={100}
/>
```

### Custom step value

Specify the step value for more precise control.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider
  labelText="Runtime memory (MB)"
  min={10}
  max={990}
  maxLabel="990 MB"
  value={100}
  step={10}
/>
```

### Light variant

Use the light variant for light backgrounds.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider
  light
  labelText="Runtime memory (MB)"
  min={10}
  max={990}
  maxLabel="990 MB"
  value={100}
  step={10}
/>
```

### Invalid state

Indicate an invalid state with the `invalid` prop. The invalid state takes
precedence over the [warning state](#warning-state) when both are set.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider
  invalid
  invalidText="Value must be a multiple of 10"
  labelText="Runtime memory (MB)"
  min={10}
  max={990}
  maxLabel="990 MB"
  value={105}
  step={10}
/>
```

### Warning state

Indicate a warning state with the `warn` prop. The warning icon appears next to
the input field, and a warning message is displayed below the slider.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider
  warn
  warnText="Value must be an increment of 5"
  labelText="Slider (must be an increment of 5)"
  min={0}
  max={100}
  value={51}
  step={5}
/>
```

### Disabled state

Disable the slider to prevent interaction.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider
  disabled
  labelText="Runtime memory (MB)"
  min={10}
  max={990}
  maxLabel="990 MB"
  value={100}
  step={10}
/>
```

### Read-only state

Set the slider to read-only to display a value without allowing changes.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Slider } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Slider
  readonly
  labelText="Runtime memory (MB)"
  min={10}
  max={990}
  maxLabel="990 MB"
  value={100}
  step={10}
/>
```

### Skeleton

Show a loading state with the skeleton variant.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SliderSkeleton } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SliderSkeleton />
```

### Skeleton without label

Show a loading state without a label.

```svelte
<script>
  import { SliderSkeleton } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<SliderSkeleton hideLabel />
```

---

### Component API

#### `Slider` props

| Prop               | Type                     | Description                                                                         | Default                             |
| ------------------ | ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `value` (Reactive) | `number`                 | Specify the value of the slider                                                     | `0`                                 |
| `ref` (Reactive)   | `null \| HTMLDivElement` | Obtain a reference to the HTML element                                              | `null`                              |
| `max`              | `number`                 | Set the maximum slider value                                                        | `100`                               |
| `maxLabel`         | `string`                 | Specify the label for the max value                                                 | `""`                                |
| `min`              | `number`                 | Set the minimum slider value                                                        | `0`                                 |
| `minLabel`         | `string`                 | Specify the label for the min value                                                 | `""`                                |
| `step`             | `number`                 | Set the step value                                                                  | `1`                                 |
| `stepMultiplier`   | `number`                 | Set the step multiplier value                                                       | `4`                                 |
| `required`         | `boolean`                | Set to `true` to require a value                                                    | `false`                             |
| `inputType`        | `string`                 | Specify the input type                                                              | `"number"`                          |
| `disabled`         | `boolean`                | Set to `true` to disable the slider                                                 | `false`                             |
| `readonly`         | `boolean`                | Set to `true` to use the read-only variant                                          | `false`                             |
| `light`            | `boolean`                | Set to `true` to enable the light variant                                           | `false`                             |
| `hideTextInput`    | `boolean`                | Set to `true` to hide the text input                                                | `false`                             |
| `fullWidth`        | `boolean`                | Set to `true` for the slider to span<br />the full width of its containing element. | `false`                             |
| `id`               | `string`                 | Set an id for the slider div element                                                | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `invalid`          | `boolean`                | Set to `true` to indicate an invalid state                                          | `false`                             |
| `invalidText`      | `string`                 | Specify the invalid state text                                                      | `""`                                |
| `warn`             | `boolean`                | Set to `true` to indicate a warning state                                           | `false`                             |
| `warnText`         | `string`                 | Specify the warning state text                                                      | `""`                                |
| `labelText`        | `string`                 | Specify the label text.<br />Alternatively, use the "labelChildren" slot.           | `""`                                |
| `hideLabel`        | `boolean`                | Set to `true` to visually hide the label text                                       | `false`                             |
| `name`             | `string`                 | Set a name for the slider element                                                   | `""`                                |

#### `Slider` slots

| Slot            | Detail                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------- |
| `labelChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `Slider` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `Slider` dispatched events

| Event       | Detail   |
| ----------- | -------- |
| `on:change` | `number` |
| `on:input`  | `number` |

#### `Slider` $$restProps

`Slider` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.

#### `SliderSkeleton` props

| Prop        | Type      | Description                          | Default |
| ----------- | --------- | ------------------------------------ | ------- |
| `hideLabel` | `boolean` | Set to `true` to hide the label text | `false` |

#### `SliderSkeleton` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `SliderSkeleton` $$restProps

`SliderSkeleton` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.


## Stack

Stacks are utility components that can be used to create a stack of elements
with a consistent gap between them. They support both vertical and horizontal
orientations.

It uses layout tokens from the Carbon Design System to define the gap between
elements. The default gap is `1`, which is equivalent to `0.125rem` (`2px`).

The `gap` scale can be set from `0` to `13`:

| Gap    | Size           |
| ------ | -------------- |
| **0**  | 0 (no gap)     |
| **1**  | 0.125rem (2px) |
| **2**  | 0.25rem (4px)  |
| **3**  | 0.5rem (8px)   |
| **4**  | 0.75rem (12px) |
| **5**  | 1rem (16px)    |
| **6**  | 1.5rem (24px)  |
| **7**  | 2rem (32px)    |
| **8**  | 2.5rem (40px)  |
| **9**  | 3rem (48px)    |
| **10** | 4rem (64px)    |
| **11** | 5rem (80px)    |
| **12** | 6rem (96px)    |
| **13** | 10rem (160px)  |

Alternatively, you can specify an arbitrary `gap` value (e.g., `200px`). You can
also set `gap` to `0` to omit any gap class.

### Vertical (gap 0)

Setting `gap` to `0` omits any gap class, resulting in no spacing between items.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={0}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 1)

By default, the stack has a vertical orientation and a gap scale of `1` (2px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 2)

Gap scale of `2` (4px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={2}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 3)

Gap scale of `3` (8px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={3}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 4)

Gap scale of `4` (12px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={4}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 5)

Gap scale of `5` (16px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={5}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 6)

Gap scale of `6` (24px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={6}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 7)

Gap scale of `7` (32px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={7}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 8)

Gap scale of `8` (40px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={8}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 9)

Gap scale of `9` (48px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={9}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 10)

Gap scale of `10` (64px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={10}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 11)

Gap scale of `11` (80px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={11}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 12)

Gap scale of `12` (96px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={12}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (gap 13)

Gap scale of `13` (160px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap={13}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical inline

Set `inline` to `true` to use `display: inline-flex` instead of `display: flex`.
This causes the stack to shrink to fit its content width instead of spanning the
full width of its container.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack inline gap={3}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (custom value)

Use an arbitrary gap value (e.g., 22px) instead of the scale.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack gap="22px">
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical (custom element)

By default, the stack uses a `div` element. You can specify a different element
using the `tag` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack tag="ul" gap={3}>
  <li>Item 1</li>
  <li>Item 2</li>
  <li>Item 3</li>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal (gap 0)

Setting `gap` to `0` omits any gap class, resulting in no spacing between items.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" gap={0}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal (gap 1)

Set the `orientation` prop to `horizontal` to create a horizontal stack. Gap
scale of `1` (2px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal">
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal (gap 2)

Gap scale of `2` (4px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" gap={2}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal (gap 3)

Gap scale of `3` (8px).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" gap={3}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal (gap 4)

Gap scale of `4` (12px).

```svelte

</script>

  <div>Item 1</div>
```

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
```

```svelte
<Stack orientation="horizontal" gap={13}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>

```

### Horizontal (custom value)

Use an arbitrary gap value (e.g., 22px) instead of the scale.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" gap="22px">
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal (custom element)

By default, the stack uses a `div` element. You can specify a different element
using the `tag` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" tag="ul" gap={3}>
  <li>Item 1</li>
  <li>Item 2</li>
  <li>Item 3</li>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal align start

Aligns items to the start of the cross-axis. For horizontal stacks, this aligns
items to the top. The default `align` value is `"stretch"`, which stretches
items to fill the container height.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" align="start" gap={2} style="height: 120px">
  <div style="height: 40px">Short</div>
  <div style="height: 80px">Taller item</div>
  <div style="height: 50px">Short</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal align center

Aligns items to the center of the cross-axis. For horizontal stacks, this
centers items vertically.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" align="center" gap={2} style="height: 120px">
  <div style="height: 40px; min-width: 60px">Short</div>
  <div style="height: 80px; min-width: 80px">Taller item</div>
  <div style="height: 50px; min-width: 60px">Short</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal align end

Aligns items to the end of the cross-axis. For horizontal stacks, this aligns
items to the bottom.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" align="end" gap={2} style="height: 120px">
  <div style="height: 40px">Short</div>
  <div style="height: 80px">Taller item</div>
  <div style="height: 50px">Short</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal align baseline

Aligns items along their baseline.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" align="baseline" gap={2}>
  <div style="font-size: 2rem; line-height: 1">Large</div>
  <div style="font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1">Medium</div>
  <div style="font-size: 0.75rem; line-height: 1">Small</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical align start

For vertical stacks, `align="start"` aligns items to the start of the cross-axis
(left).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="vertical" align="start" gap={2}>
  <div style="width: 80px">Narrow</div>
  <div style="width: 150px">Wider item</div>
  <div style="width: 100px">Medium</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical align center

For vertical stacks, `align="center"` centers items horizontally.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="vertical" align="center" gap={2}>
  <div style="width: 80px">Narrow</div>
  <div style="width: 150px">Wider item</div>
  <div style="width: 100px">Medium</div>
</Stack>
```

### Vertical align end

For vertical stacks, `align="end"` aligns items to the end of the cross-axis
(right).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="vertical" align="end" gap={2}>
  <div style="width: 80px">Narrow</div>
  <div style="width: 150px">Wider item</div>
  <div style="width: 100px">Medium</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal justify center

Aligns items to the center of the main axis. The default `justify` value is
`"start"`, which aligns items to the start of the main axis.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" justify="center" gap={2}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal justify end

Aligns items to the end of the main axis.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" justify="end" gap={2}>
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal justify space-between

Distributes items evenly with the first item at the start and the last item at
the end.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" justify="space-between">
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal justify space-around

Distributes items evenly with equal space around each item.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" justify="space-around">
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Horizontal justify space-evenly

Distributes items evenly with equal space between each item.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack orientation="horizontal" justify="space-evenly">
  <div>Item 1</div>
  <div>Item 2</div>
  <div>Item 3</div>
</Stack>
```

### Combined align and justify

Use both `align` and `justify` to center content on both axes.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Stack align="center" justify="center" gap={4}>
  <div>Centered</div>
  <div>Content</div>
</Stack>
```

---

### Component API

#### `Stack` props

| Prop          | Type                                                                                  | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Default      |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------ |
| `gap`         | `StackScale \| string`                                                                | Specify the gap between items in the stack.<br />The scale maps to Carbon layout values.<br />Use 0 to omit any gap class.<br />Alternatively, specify a custom value (e.g., "200px" or "1.5rem").<br />Custom values _must_ be a string. | `1`          |
| `orientation` | `"vertical" \| "horizontal"`                                                          | Specify the orientation of the stack.                                                                                                                                                                                                     | `"vertical"` |
| `align`       | `"start" \| "center" \| "end" \| "stretch" \| "baseline"`                             | Specify the cross-axis alignment of items in the stack.                                                                                                                                                                                   | `"stretch"`  |
| `justify`     | `"start" \| "center" \| "end" \| "space-between" \| "space-around" \| "space-evenly"` | Specify the main-axis alignment of items in the stack.                                                                                                                                                                                    | `"start"`    |
