| `maxlength`        | `number`                   | Specify the `maxlength` input attribute               | `5`                                  |
| `light`            | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to enable the light variant             | `false`                              |
| `disabled`         | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to disable the input                    | `false`                              |
| `labelText`        | `string`                   | Specify the label text                                | `""`                                 |
| `hideLabel`        | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to visually hide the label text         | `false`                              |
| `invalid`          | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to indicate an invalid state            | `false`                              |
| `invalidText`      | `string`                   | Specify the invalid state text                        | `""`                                 |
| `id`               | `string`                   | Set an id for the input element                       | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}`  |
| `name`             | `string`                   | Specify a name attribute for the input.               | _undefined_                          |

#### `TimePicker` slots

| Slot            | Detail                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------- |
| `default`       | `Record<string, never>` |
| `labelChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `TimePicker` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:blur`       |
| `on:change`     |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:focus`      |
| `on:input`      |
| `on:keydown`    |
| `on:keyup`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |
| `on:paste`      |

#### `TimePicker` $$restProps

`TimePicker` spreads `$$restProps` to the `input` element.

#### `TimePickerSelect` props

| Prop               | Type                        | Description                                      | Default                             |
| ------------------ | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------- |
| `value` (Reactive) | `number \| string`          | Specify the select value.                        | `""`                                |
| `ref` (Reactive)   | `null \| HTMLSelectElement` | Obtain a reference to the select HTML element    | `null`                              |
| `disabled`         | `boolean`                   | Set to `true` to disable the select              | `false`                             |
| `iconDescription`  | `string`                    | Specify the ARIA label for the chevron icon      | `"Open list of options"`            |
| `labelText`        | `string`                    | Specify the label text                           | `""`                                |
| `id`               | `string`                    | Set an id for the select element                 | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `name`             | `string`                    | Specify a name attribute for the select element. | _undefined_                         |

#### `TimePickerSelect` slots

| Slot            | Detail                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------- |
| `default`       | `Record<string, never>` |
| `labelChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `TimePickerSelect` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `TimePickerSelect` $$restProps

`TimePickerSelect` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.

#### `SelectItem` props

| Prop       | Type      | Description                                                                         | Default     |
| ---------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| `value`    | `Value`   | Specify the option value.                                                           | `""`        |
| `text`     | `string`  | Specify the option text.<br />If not specified, the value will be used as the text. | _undefined_ |
| `hidden`   | `boolean` | Set to `true` to hide the option                                                    | `false`     |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | Set to `true` to disable the option                                                 | `false`     |
| `class`    | `string`  | Specify the class of the `option` element.                                          | _undefined_ |
| `style`    | `string`  | Specify the style of the `option` element.                                          | _undefined_ |


## ToastNotification

Toast notifications display non-modal, time-based messages that appear at the
top of the screen and automatically disappear. They support various notification
types, custom content, and accessibility features.

For an imperative API to manage multiple toast notifications with automatic
queue management, see [NotificationQueue](NotificationQueue).

See
[detailed usage](https://carbondesignsystem.com/components/notification/usage).  
See
also: [InlineNotification](InlineNotification)

### Default

Display a basic error notification with title, subtitle, and timestamp by
default.

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification
  title="Error"
  subtitle="An internal server error occurred."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
```

### Autoclose

Specify the `timeout` prop to automatically close the notification after a
specified duration (in milliseconds).

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import { fade } from "svelte/transition";

  let timeout = undefined;
  $: showNotification = timeout !== undefined;
</script>

<Button
  disabled={showNotification}
  on:click={() => {
    timeout = 3_000; // 3 seconds
  }}
>
  Show notification
</Button>

{#if showNotification}
  <div transition:fade>
    <ToastNotification
      {timeout}
      kind="success"
      title="Success"
      subtitle="This notification will autoclose in {timeout.toLocaleString()} ms."
      caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
      on:close={(e) => {
        timeout = undefined;
        console.log(e.detail.timeout); // true if closed via timeout
      }}
    />
  </div>
{/if}
```

### Prevent default close behavior

Prevent the default close behavior using `e.preventDefault()` in the `on:close`
event handler.

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification
  title="Error"
  subtitle="An internal server error occurred."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
  on:close={(e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    // custom close logic (e.g., transitions)
  }}
/>

## Reusable notification Control the notification visibility by binding to the
`open` prop. This allows you to show, hide, and reuse the same notification
component with different messages. The `open` prop defaults to `true`.
```

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    ButtonSet,
    Stack,
    ToastNotification,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let open = false;
  let kind = "error";
  let title = "Error";
  let subtitle = "An internal server error occurred.";
  let caption = new Date().toLocaleString();

  function showError() {
    kind = "error";
    title = "Error";
    subtitle = "An internal server error occurred.";
    caption = new Date().toLocaleString();
    open = true;
  }

  function showSuccess() {
    kind = "success";
    title = "Success";
    subtitle = "Your settings have been saved.";
    caption = new Date().toLocaleString();
    open = true;
  }

  function showWarning() {
    kind = "warning";
    title = "Warning";
    subtitle = "Please review your changes before continuing.";
    caption = new Date().toLocaleString();
    open = true;
  }
</script>

<Stack gap={5}>
  <ButtonSet>
    <Button on:click={showSuccess}>Show success</Button>
    <Button kind="ghost" on:click={showWarning}>Show warning</Button>
    <Button kind="danger-ghost" on:click={showError}>Show error</Button>
  </ButtonSet>

  <ToastNotification bind:open {kind} {title} {subtitle} {caption} />
</Stack>
```

### Full width

Set `fullWidth` to `true` for the notification to span the full width of its
container.

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification
  fullWidth
  title="Error"
  subtitle="An internal server error occurred."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
```

### Slottable title, subtitle, caption

Customize the notification content using slots for more flexibility.

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification>
  <strong slot="titleChildren">Error: </strong>
  <strong slot="subtitleChildren">An internal server error occurred.</strong>
  <strong slot="captionChildren">{new Date().toLocaleString()}</strong>
</ToastNotification>
```

### Optional subtitle and caption

Subtitle and caption are optional and only render when provided.

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification subtitle="An internal server error occurred." />
<ToastNotification
  title="Error"
  subtitle="An internal server error occurred."
/>
<ToastNotification
  subtitle="An internal server error occurred."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification title="Success" caption={new Date().toLocaleString()} />
```

### Accessible icon descriptions

Provide custom descriptions for icons to improve accessibility.

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification
  title="错误"
  subtitle="发生内部服务器错误"
  statusIconDescription="错误图标"
  closeButtonDescription="关闭通知"
/>
```

### Hidden close button

Create a persistent notification by hiding the close button.

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification
  hideCloseButton
  kind="warning"
  title="Scheduled maintenance"
  subtitle="Maintenance will last 2-4 hours."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
```

### Notification variants

The component supports different notification types:

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification
  kind="error"
  title="Error"
  subtitle="An internal server error occurred."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  kind="info"
  title="New updates"
  subtitle="Restart to get the latest updates."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  kind="info-square"
  title="New updates"
  subtitle="Restart to get the latest updates."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  kind="success"
  title="Success"
  subtitle="Your settings have been saved."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  kind="warning"
  title="Scheduled maintenance"
  subtitle="Maintenance will last 2-4 hours."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  kind="warning-alt"
  title="Scheduled maintenance"
  subtitle="Maintenance will last 2-4 hours."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
```

### Low contrast

Use low contrast variants for less prominent notifications.

```svelte
<script>
  import { ToastNotification } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<ToastNotification
  lowContrast
  kind="error"
  title="Error"
  subtitle="An internal server error occurred."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  lowContrast
  kind="info"
  title="New updates"
  subtitle="Restart to get the latest updates."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  lowContrast
  kind="info-square"
  title="New updates"
  subtitle="Restart to get the latest updates."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  lowContrast
  kind="success"
  title="Success"
  subtitle="Your settings have been saved."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  lowContrast
  kind="warning"
  title="Scheduled maintenance"
  subtitle="Maintenance will last 2-4 hours."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
<ToastNotification
  lowContrast
  kind="warning-alt"
  title="Scheduled maintenance"
  subtitle="Maintenance will last 2-4 hours."
  caption={new Date().toLocaleString()}
/>
```

---

### Component API

#### `ToastNotification` props

| Prop                     | Type                                                                            | Description                                                                               | Default                |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------- |
| `open` (Reactive)        | `boolean`                                                                       | Set to `true` to show the notification, `false` to hide it.                               | `true`                 |
| `kind`                   | `"error" \| "info" \| "info-square" \| "success" \| "warning" \| "warning-alt"` | Specify the kind of notification.                                                         | `"error"`              |
| `lowContrast`            | `boolean`                                                                       | Set to `true` to use the low contrast variant                                             | `false`                |
| `timeout`                | `number`                                                                        | Set the timeout duration (ms) to hide the notification after opening it                   | `0`                    |
| `role`                   | `string`                                                                        | Set the `role` attribute                                                                  | `"alert"`              |
| `title`                  | `string`                                                                        | Specify the title text                                                                    | `""`                   |
| `subtitle`               | `string`                                                                        | Specify the subtitle text                                                                 | `""`                   |
| `caption`                | `string`                                                                        | Specify the caption text                                                                  | `""`                   |
| `statusIconDescription`  | `string`                                                                        | Specify the ARIA label for the status icon.                                               | `${kind} icon`         |
| `closeButtonDescription` | `string`                                                                        | Specify the ARIA label for the close button                                               | `"Close notification"` |
| `hideCloseButton`        | `boolean`                                                                       | Set to `true` to hide the close button                                                    | `false`                |
| `fullWidth`              | `boolean`                                                                       | Set to `true` for the notification to span<br />the full width of its containing element. | `false`                |

#### `ToastNotification` slots

| Slot               | Detail                  |
| ------------------ | ----------------------- |
| `default`          | `Record<string, never>` |
| `captionChildren`  | `Record<string, never>` |
| `subtitleChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |
| `titleChildren`    | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `ToastNotification` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `ToastNotification` dispatched events

| Event      | Detail                              |
| ---------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `on:close` | `{<br />  timeout: boolean;<br />}` |

#### `ToastNotification` $$restProps

`ToastNotification` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.


## Toggle

Toggles provide a switch-like control that allows users to turn options on or
off. They support custom labels, different sizes, and various states to match
your application's needs.

### Default

Display a basic toggle in its untoggled state by default.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Toggle } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toggle labelText="Push notifications" />
```

### Toggled

Set `toggled` to `true` to display the toggle in its on state.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Toggle } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toggle labelText="Push notifications" toggled />
```

### Reactive example

Use two-way binding to control the toggle state programmatically.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, Stack, Toggle } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let toggled = true;
</script>

<Stack gap={6}>
  <Toggle labelText="Push notifications" bind:toggled />
  <div>
    <Button kind="tertiary" size="small" on:click={() => (toggled = !toggled)}>
      Toggle
      {toggled ? "off" : "on"}
    </Button>
  </div>
</Stack>
```

### on:toggle event

Listen for toggle state changes using the `on:toggle` event.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Toggle, ToggleSkeleton } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toggle
  labelText="Push notifications"
  on:toggle={(e) => console.log(e.detail)}
/>

## Hidden label text Set `hideLabel` to `true` to visually hide the label while
maintaining accessibility.

<Toggle labelText="Push notifications" hideLabel />

## Custom labels Customize the toggle labels using `labelA` and `labelB` props.

<Toggle labelText="Push notifications" labelA="No" labelB="Yes" />

## Slottable labels Use slots to customize the toggle labels with additional
styling or content.

<Toggle labelText="Push notifications">
  <span slot="labelA" style="color: red">No</span>
  <span slot="labelB" style="color: green">Yes</span>
</Toggle>

## Disabled state Set `disabled` to `true` to prevent user interaction.

<Toggle labelText="Push notifications" disabled />

## Small size Use the small size variant by setting `size` to `"sm"`.

<Toggle size="sm" labelText="Push notifications" />

## Skeleton Display a loading state using the skeleton component.

<ToggleSkeleton />

## Skeleton (small) Use the small size variant for the skeleton component.

<ToggleSkeleton size="sm" />
```

---

### Component API

#### `Toggle` props

| Prop                 | Type                       | Description                                      | Default                             |
| -------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------- |
| `toggled` (Reactive) | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to toggle the checkbox input       | `false`                             |
| `ref` (Reactive)     | `null \| HTMLInputElement` | Obtain a reference to the input HTML element     | `null`                              |
| `size`               | `"default" \| "sm"`        | Specify the toggle size.                         | `"default"`                         |
| `disabled`           | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to disable checkbox input          | `false`                             |
| `labelA`             | `string`                   | Specify the label for the untoggled state        | `"Off"`                             |
| `labelB`             | `string`                   | Specify the label for the toggled state          | `"On"`                              |
| `labelText`          | `string`                   | Specify the label text                           | `""`                                |
| `hideLabel`          | `boolean`                  | Set to `true` to visually hide the label text    | `false`                             |
| `id`                 | `string`                   | Set an id for the input element                  | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `name`               | `string`                   | Specify a name attribute for the checkbox input. | _undefined_                         |

#### `Toggle` slots

| Slot            | Detail                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------- |
| `labelA`        | `Record<string, never>` |
| `labelB`        | `Record<string, never>` |
| `labelChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `Toggle` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:blur`       |
| `on:change`     |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:focus`      |
| `on:keyup`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `Toggle` dispatched events

| Event       | Detail                 |
| ----------- | ---------------------- |
| `on:toggle` | `{ toggled: boolean }` |

#### `Toggle` $$restProps

`Toggle` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.

#### `ToggleSkeleton` props

| Prop        | Type                | Description                     | Default                             |
| ----------- | ------------------- | ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `size`      | `"default" \| "sm"` | Specify the toggle size.        | `"default"`                         |
| `labelText` | `string`            | Specify the label text          | `""`                                |
| `id`        | `string`            | Set an id for the input element | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |

#### `ToggleSkeleton` slots

| Slot            | Detail                  |
| --------------- | ----------------------- |
| `labelChildren` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `ToggleSkeleton` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `ToggleSkeleton` $$restProps

`ToggleSkeleton` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.


## Toolbar

Toolbars provide a flexible container for organizing actions, search, and menu
items. They can be used standalone or as part of a data table for batch
operations and filtering.

### Default

Create a basic toolbar with content inside toolbar content.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, Toolbar, ToolbarContent } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <Button>Primary action</Button>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### Small size

Set `size="sm"` for a compact toolbar.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, Toolbar, ToolbarContent } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar size="sm">
  <ToolbarContent>
    <Button size="small">Create</Button>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### With search

Add search functionality using toolbar search.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarSearch,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <ToolbarSearch />
    <Button>Create</Button>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### With persistent search

Set `persistent` on toolbar search to keep it always expanded.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarSearch,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <ToolbarSearch persistent placeholder="Search..." />
    <Button>Create</Button>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### Reactive search

Bind to the `value` prop for reactive updates.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    ButtonSet,
    Stack,
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarSearch,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let value = "";
</script>

<Stack gap={6}>
  <Toolbar>
    <ToolbarContent>
      <ToolbarSearch bind:value placeholder="Search..." />
      <Button size="small">Create</Button>
    </ToolbarContent>
  </Toolbar>
  <Stack gap={5}>
    <div>
      <ButtonSet>
        <Button
          size="small"
          disabled={value === "products"}
          on:click={() => (value = "products")}
        >
          Set value
        </Button>
        <Button
          kind="ghost"
          size="small"
          disabled={value.length === 0}
          on:click={() => (value = "")}
        >
          Clear value
        </Button>
      </ButtonSet>
    </div>
    <div>
      <strong>Search value:</strong>
      {value}
    </div>
  </Stack>
</Stack>
```

### With menu

Add an overflow menu using toolbar menu and toolbar menu item.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarMenu,
    ToolbarMenuItem,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <ToolbarMenu>
      <ToolbarMenuItem>Restart all</ToolbarMenuItem>
      <ToolbarMenuItem href="https://cloud.ibm.com/docs"
        >API documentation</ToolbarMenuItem
      >
      <ToolbarMenuItem>Settings</ToolbarMenuItem>
    </ToolbarMenu>
    <Button>Create</Button>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### Menu with divider and danger item

Use `hasDivider` to add a separator and `danger` for destructive actions.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarMenu,
    ToolbarMenuItem,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <ToolbarMenu>
      <ToolbarMenuItem>Restart all</ToolbarMenuItem>
      <ToolbarMenuItem href="https://cloud.ibm.com/docs"
        >API documentation</ToolbarMenuItem
      >
      <ToolbarMenuItem hasDivider danger>Stop all</ToolbarMenuItem>
    </ToolbarMenu>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### Menu with primary focus

Set `primaryFocus` on a menu item to focus it when opening the dropdown.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarMenu,
    ToolbarMenuItem,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <ToolbarMenu>
      <ToolbarMenuItem>Restart all</ToolbarMenuItem>
      <ToolbarMenuItem href="https://cloud.ibm.com/docs"
        >API documentation</ToolbarMenuItem
      >
      <ToolbarMenuItem primaryFocus hasDivider danger>Stop all</ToolbarMenuItem>
    </ToolbarMenu>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### Complete toolbar

Combine search, menu, and buttons for a full-featured toolbar.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarMenu,
    ToolbarMenuItem,
    ToolbarSearch,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <ToolbarSearch />
    <ToolbarMenu>
      <ToolbarMenuItem primaryFocus>Restart all</ToolbarMenuItem>
      <ToolbarMenuItem href="https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/loadbalancer-service">
        API documentation
      </ToolbarMenuItem>
      <ToolbarMenuItem hasDivider danger>Stop all</ToolbarMenuItem>
    </ToolbarMenu>
    <Button>Create balancer</Button>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### With multiple actions

Stack multiple action buttons in the toolbar.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarSearch,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <ToolbarSearch persistent />
    <Button kind="secondary" size="small">Export</Button>
    <Button size="small">Create</Button>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### Standalone usage

> [!NOTE] **Toolbar** can be used independently of DataTable. It provides its
> own context for child components like **ToolbarMenu** and **ToolbarSearch**.

The toolbar components work standalone for general-purpose layouts:

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarContent,
    ToolbarMenu,
    ToolbarMenuItem,
    ToolbarSearch,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Toolbar>
  <ToolbarContent>
    <ToolbarSearch placeholder="Search products..." />
    <ToolbarMenu>
      <ToolbarMenuItem>Filter by category</ToolbarMenuItem>
      <ToolbarMenuItem>Sort by price</ToolbarMenuItem>
    </ToolbarMenu>
    <Button>Add product</Button>
  </ToolbarContent>
</Toolbar>
```

### Batch actions

Toolbar batch actions display batch actions when items are selected.

Although intended for use with [DataTable](/components/datatable), this
component can be used standalone if `selectedIds` is provided. This prop does
not need to be provided if using with a data table.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Checkbox,
    Stack,
    Toolbar,
    ToolbarBatchActions,
    ToolbarContent,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Add from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Add.svelte";
  import TrashCan from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/TrashCan.svelte";

  let items = [
    { id: 1, name: "Item 1", checked: false },
    { id: 2, name: "Item 2", checked: false },
    { id: 3, name: "Item 3", checked: false },
  ];
  let nextId = items.length + 1;

  $: selectedIds = items.filter((item) => item.checked).map((item) => item.id);
</script>

<Stack gap={6}>
  <Toolbar>
    <ToolbarBatchActions
      {selectedIds}
      on:cancel={() => {
        items = items.map((item) => ({ ...item, checked: false }));
      }}
    >
      <Button
        kind="danger"
        icon={TrashCan}
        on:click={() => {
          items = items.filter((item) => !item.checked);
        }}
      >
        Delete
      </Button>
    </ToolbarBatchActions>
    <ToolbarContent>
      <Button
        icon={Add}
        size="small"
        on:click={() => {
          items = [
            ...items,
            { id: nextId, name: `Item ${nextId}`, checked: false },
          ];
          nextId++;
        }}
      >
        Add item
      </Button>
    </ToolbarContent>
  </Toolbar>
  <Stack>
    {#each items as item (item.id)}
      <Checkbox labelText={item.name} bind:checked={item.checked} />
    {/each}
  </Stack>
</Stack>
```

---

### Component API

#### `Toolbar` props

| Prop   | Type                | Description               | Default     |
| ------ | ------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------- |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "default"` | Specify the toolbar size. | `"default"` |

#### `Toolbar` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `Toolbar` $$restProps

`Toolbar` spreads `$$restProps` to the `section` element.

#### `ToolbarContent` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `ToolbarSearch` props

| Prop                        | Type                                                          | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Default |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| `value` (Reactive)          | `number \| string`                                            | Specify the value of the search input.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | `""`    |
| `expanded` (Reactive)       | `boolean`                                                     | Set to `true` to expand the search bar                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | `false` |
| `filteredRowIds` (Reactive) | `ReadonlyArray<Row["id"]>`                                    | The filtered row ids.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | `[]`    |
| `ref` (Reactive)            | `null \| HTMLInputElement`                                    | Obtain a reference to the input HTML element.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | `null`  |
| `persistent`                | `boolean`                                                     | Set to `true` to keep the search bar expanded                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | `false` |
| `disabled`                  | `boolean`                                                     | Set to `true` to disable the search bar                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | `false` |
| `shouldFilterRows`          | `boolean \| ((row: Row, value: number \| string) => boolean)` | Set to `true` to filter table rows using the search value.<br /><br />If `true`, the default search excludes `id`, `cells` fields and<br />only does a basic comparison on string and number type cell values.<br /><br />To implement your own client-side filtering, pass a function<br />that accepts a row and value and returns a boolean. | `false` |
| `tabindex`                  | `number \| string \| undefined`                               | Specify the tabindex                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | `"0"`   |

#### `ToolbarSearch` forwarded events

| Event        |
| ------------ |
| `on:blur`    |
| `on:change`  |
| `on:clear`   |
| `on:focus`   |
| `on:input`   |
| `on:keydown` |
| `on:keyup`   |
| `on:paste`   |

#### `ToolbarSearch` $$restProps

`ToolbarSearch` spreads `$$restProps` to the `input` element.

#### `ToolbarMenu` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `ToolbarMenu` $$restProps

`ToolbarMenu` spreads `$$restProps` to the `OverflowMenu` component.

#### `ToolbarMenuItem` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `ToolbarMenuItem` forwarded events

| Event        |
| ------------ |
| `on:click`   |
| `on:keydown` |

#### `ToolbarMenuItem` $$restProps

`ToolbarMenuItem` spreads `$$restProps` to the `OverflowMenuItem` component.

#### `ToolbarBatchActions` props

| Prop                  | Type                                | Description                                                                                                       | Default     |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| `active` (Reactive)   | `undefined \| boolean`              | Use a boolean to show or hide the toolbar.                                                                        | _undefined_ |
| `formatTotalSelected` | `(totalSelected: number) => string` | Override the total items selected text.                                                                           | _undefined_ |
| `selectedIds`         | `ReadonlyArray<Id>`                 | Specify the selected IDs for standalone usage.<br />This is unnecessary if using this component with `DataTable`. | `[]`        |

#### `ToolbarBatchActions` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |
| `cancel`  | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `ToolbarBatchActions` dispatched events

| Event       | Detail |
| ----------- | ------ |
| `on:cancel` | `null` |

#### `ToolbarBatchActions` $$restProps

`ToolbarBatchActions` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.


## Tooltip

Tooltips display contextual information when users hover over or focus on an
element. They support various directions, alignments, and interactive content to
provide additional context or guidance.

### Default

Display a tooltip triggered by the default information icon.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip>
  <p>Resources are provisioned based on your account's organization.</p>
</Tooltip>
```

### With trigger text

Use custom trigger text instead of the default icon.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip triggerText="Resource list">
  <p>Resources are provisioned based on your account's organization.</p>
</Tooltip>
```

### Reactive example

Control the tooltip state programmatically.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, Stack, Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let open = true;
</script>

<Stack gap={12}>
  <Tooltip bind:open triggerText="Resource list" align="start">
    <p>Resources are provisioned based on your account's organization.</p>
  </Tooltip>
  <div>
    <Button kind="tertiary" size="small" on:click={() => (open = !open)}>
      {open ? "Close tooltip" : "Open tooltip"}
    </Button>
  </div>
</Stack>
```

### Directions

Position the tooltip in different directions using the `direction` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip triggerText="Top" direction="top"><p>Top</p></Tooltip>
<Tooltip triggerText="Right" direction="right"><p>Right</p></Tooltip>
<Tooltip triggerText="Bottom" direction="bottom"><p>Bottom</p></Tooltip>
<Tooltip triggerText="Left" direction="left"><p>Left</p></Tooltip>
```

### Alignment

Align the tooltip content using the `align` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip triggerText="Start" align="start"><p>Start</p></Tooltip>
<Tooltip triggerText="End" align="end"><p>End</p></Tooltip>
<Tooltip triggerText="Center" align="center"><p>Center</p></Tooltip>
```

### Interactive

Add interactive elements like links and buttons using the tooltip footer.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Link,
    Tooltip,
    TooltipFooter,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip triggerText="Resource list">
  <p>Resources are provisioned based on your account's organization.</p>
  <TooltipFooter selectorPrimaryFocus="#d">
    <Link href="/">Learn more</Link>
    <Button id="d" size="small">Manage</Button>
  </TooltipFooter>
</Tooltip>
```

### Custom icon (component)

Use a custom icon component with the `icon` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Catalog from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Catalog.svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip triggerText="Resource list" icon={Catalog}>
  <p>Resources are provisioned based on your account's organization.</p>
</Tooltip>
```

### Custom icon (slot)

Customize the icon using the `icon` slot for more flexibility.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip triggerText="Resource list">
  <div
    slot="icon"
    style="width: 1rem; height: 1rem; outline: 1px solid red;"
  ></div>
  <p>Resources are provisioned based on your account's organization.</p>
</Tooltip>
```

### Custom delay

The tooltip has a hover delay to avoid accidental opening. The default enter
delay is 100ms and default leave delay is 300ms.

Customize the enter and leave delay using `enterDelayMs` and `leaveDelayMs`.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip triggerText="Slow open" enterDelayMs={500} leaveDelayMs={200}>
  <p>This tooltip has a 500ms enter delay and a 200ms leave delay.</p>
</Tooltip>
```

### Hidden icon

Hide the icon while keeping the tooltip functionality using `hideIcon`.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip hideIcon triggerText="Resource list">
  <p>Resources are provisioned based on your account's organization.</p>
</Tooltip>
```

### Portal tooltip

Use `portalTooltip` to render the tooltip in a portal, preventing it from being
clipped by `overflow: hidden` containers. This is automatically enabled when the
tooltip is inside a `Modal`.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip portalTooltip triggerText="Portalled">
  <p>This tooltip is rendered in a portal.</p>
</Tooltip>
```

### Portal tooltip (hidden icon)

Portal tooltips also work with `hideIcon`.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip portalTooltip hideIcon triggerText="Portalled (hidden icon)">
  <p>This tooltip is rendered in a portal without the icon.</p>
</Tooltip>
```

### Portal tooltip directions

Position portalled tooltips in different directions.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Tooltip } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<Tooltip portalTooltip triggerText="Top" direction="top"><p>Top</p></Tooltip>
<Tooltip portalTooltip triggerText="Right" direction="right"
  ><p>Right</p></Tooltip
>
<Tooltip portalTooltip triggerText="Bottom" direction="bottom"
  ><p>Bottom</p></Tooltip
>
<Tooltip portalTooltip triggerText="Left" direction="left"><p>Left</p></Tooltip>
```

### Inside a modal

When a tooltip is used inside a [Modal](/components/Modal), it is automatically
rendered in a portal so it is not clipped by the modal's overflow or z-index
stacking. No need to set `portalTooltip` in this case.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Modal,
    Portal,
    Stack,
    Tooltip,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let open = false;
</script>

<Button on:click={() => (open = true)}>Open modal</Button>

<Portal>
  <Modal
    bind:open
    size="sm"
    modalHeading="Tooltip in modal"
    primaryButtonText="Done"
    secondaryButtonText="Cancel"
    on:click:button--secondary={() => (open = false)}
  >
    <Tooltip triggerText="Top" direction="top"><p>Top</p></Tooltip>
    <Tooltip triggerText="Right" direction="right"><p>Right</p></Tooltip>
    <Tooltip triggerText="Bottom" direction="bottom"><p>Bottom</p></Tooltip>
    <Tooltip triggerText="Left" direction="left"><p>Left</p></Tooltip>
  </Modal>
</Portal>
```

---

### Component API

#### `Tooltip` props

| Prop                    | Type                                     | Description                                                                                                                                                                               | Default                             |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `open` (Reactive)       | `boolean`                                | Set to `true` to open the tooltip.                                                                                                                                                        | `false`                             |
| `ref` (Reactive)        | `null \| HTMLDivElement`                 | Obtain a reference to the trigger text HTML element                                                                                                                                       | `null`                              |
| `refTooltip` (Reactive) | `null \| HTMLDivElement`                 | Obtain a reference to the tooltip HTML element                                                                                                                                            | `null`                              |
| `refIcon` (Reactive)    | `null \| HTMLDivElement`                 | Obtain a reference to the icon HTML element                                                                                                                                               | `null`                              |
| `align`                 | `"start" \| "center" \| "end"`           | Set the alignment of the tooltip relative to the icon.                                                                                                                                    | `"center"`                          |
| `direction`             | `"top" \| "right" \| "bottom" \| "left"` | Set the direction of the tooltip relative to the button.                                                                                                                                  | `"bottom"`                          |
| `hideIcon`              | `boolean`                                | Set to `true` to hide the tooltip icon.                                                                                                                                                   | `false`                             |
| `icon`                  | `Icon`                                   | Specify the icon to render for the tooltip button.<br />Defaults to `<Information />`.                                                                                                    | `Information`                       |
| `iconDescription`       | `string`                                 | Specify the ARIA label for the tooltip button                                                                                                                                             | `""`                                |
| `iconName`              | `string`                                 | Specify the icon name attribute                                                                                                                                                           | `""`                                |
| `tabindex`              | `number \| string \| undefined`          | Set the button tabindex                                                                                                                                                                   | `"0"`                               |
| `tooltipId`             | `string`                                 | Set an id for the tooltip.                                                                                                                                                                | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `triggerId`             | `string`                                 | Set an id for the tooltip button.                                                                                                                                                         | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `triggerText`           | `string`                                 | Set the tooltip button text                                                                                                                                                               | `""`                                |
| `enterDelayMs`          | `number`                                 | Specify the duration in milliseconds to delay before displaying the tooltip.                                                                                                              | `100`                               |
| `leaveDelayMs`          | `number`                                 | Specify the duration in milliseconds to delay before hiding the tooltip.                                                                                                                  | `300`                               |
| `portalTooltip`         | `boolean \| undefined`                   | Set to `true` to render the tooltip in a portal,<br />preventing it from being clipped by `overflow: hidden` containers.<br />By default, the tooltip is portalled when inside a `Modal`. | _undefined_                         |

#### `Tooltip` slots

| Slot          | Detail                  |
| ------------- | ----------------------- |
| `default`     | `Record<string, never>` |
| `icon`        | `Record<string, never>` |
| `triggerText` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `Tooltip` forwarded events

| Event          |
| -------------- |
| `on:click`     |
| `on:mousedown` |

#### `Tooltip` dispatched events

| Event      | Detail |
| ---------- | ------ |
| `on:close` | `null` |
| `on:open`  | `null` |

#### `Tooltip` $$restProps

`Tooltip` spreads `$$restProps` to the `div` element.

#### `TooltipFooter` props

| Prop                   | Type     | Description                                                                 | Default                             |
| ---------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `selectorPrimaryFocus` | `string` | Specify a selector to be focused inside the footer when opening the tooltip | `"a[href], button:not([disabled])"` |

#### `TooltipFooter` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |


## TooltipDefinition

Tooltip definitions provide inline definitions for terms or concepts. They
display a tooltip when users hover over or focus on the defined term, making
them ideal for providing additional context without cluttering the interface.

### Default

Display a basic definition tooltip with the default bottom-center alignment.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition
  tooltipText="IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York."
>
  Armonk
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Direction

By default, the tooltip is positioned to the bottom.

Position the tooltip in different directions using the `direction` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition direction="top" tooltipText="Top">Top</TooltipDefinition>
<TooltipDefinition direction="bottom" tooltipText="Bottom">
  Bottom
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Alignment

By default, the tooltip is aligned to the center.

Align the tooltip content using the `align` prop.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition align="start" tooltipText="Start">Start</TooltipDefinition>
<TooltipDefinition align="center" tooltipText="Center">
  Center
</TooltipDefinition>
<TooltipDefinition align="end" tooltipText="End">End</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Click to open

Set `clickToOpen` to open the tooltip on click instead of on hover.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition
  clickToOpen
  tooltipText="IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York."
>
  Armonk
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Open by default

Use the `open` prop to open the tooltip by default.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition
  open
  tooltipText="IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York."
>
  Armonk
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Programmatic usage

Control the tooltip state programmatically using `bind:open`.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, Stack, TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let open = true;
</script>

<Stack gap={12}>
  <div>
    <TooltipDefinition
      align="start"
      bind:open
      tooltipText="IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York."
    >
      Armonk
    </TooltipDefinition>
  </div>
  <div>
    <Button kind="tertiary" size="small" on:click={() => (open = !open)}>
      {open ? "Close tooltip" : "Open tooltip"}
    </Button>
  </div>
</Stack>
```

### Dispatched events

The component dispatches `open` and `close` events.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let events = [];
</script>

<TooltipDefinition
  align="start"
  tooltipText="IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York."
  on:open={() => (events = [...events, "open"])}
  on:close={() => (events = [...events, "close"])}
>
  Dispatched events: {events.join(", ")}
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Custom delay

Use `enterDelayMs` and `leaveDelayMs` to customize the delay before showing and
hiding the tooltip on hover. The defaults are 100ms and 300ms, respectively.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition
  tooltipText="Slow to appear"
  enterDelayMs={500}
  leaveDelayMs={500}
>
  Armonk
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Custom tooltip slot

Use the `tooltip` slot to customize the tooltip content with additional styling
or structure.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition>
  Armonk
  <span slot="tooltip" style="color: red">
    IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York.
  </span>
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Portal tooltip

Use `portalTooltip` to render the tooltip in a portal, preventing it from being
clipped by `overflow: hidden` containers. This is automatically enabled when the
tooltip is inside a `Modal`. The `align` prop is respected when portalled,
matching non-portal positioning.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition
  portalTooltip
  tooltipText="IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York."
>
  Armonk
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Portal (direction)

Definition tooltips support `top` and `bottom` only. With `portalTooltip`,
`direction` behaves the same as the default CSS path.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition portalTooltip direction="top" tooltipText="Top">
  Top
</TooltipDefinition>
<TooltipDefinition portalTooltip direction="bottom" tooltipText="Bottom">
  Bottom
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Portal (alignment)

Portalled tooltips use the same `align` prop as the non-portal path: horizontal
placement along the anchor for `top` and `bottom`.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipDefinition } from "carbon-components-svelte";
</script>

<TooltipDefinition portalTooltip align="start" tooltipText="Start">
  Start
</TooltipDefinition>
<TooltipDefinition portalTooltip align="center" tooltipText="Center">
  Center
</TooltipDefinition>
<TooltipDefinition portalTooltip align="end" tooltipText="End">
  End
</TooltipDefinition>
```

### Inside a modal

When a tooltip definition is used inside a [Modal](/components/Modal), it is
automatically rendered in a portal so it is not clipped by the modal's overflow
or z-index stacking. Set `portalTooltip` to explicitly control this behavior.

```svelte
<script>
  import {
    Button,
    Modal,
    Portal,
    TooltipDefinition,
  } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let open = false;
</script>

<Button on:click={() => (open = true)}>Open modal</Button>

<Portal>
  <Modal
    bind:open
    size="sm"
    modalHeading="TooltipDefinition in modal"
    primaryButtonText="Done"
    secondaryButtonText="Cancel"
    on:click:button--secondary={() => (open = false)}
  >
    <TooltipDefinition
      direction="top"
      tooltipText="IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York."
    >
      Armonk (top)
    </TooltipDefinition>
    <TooltipDefinition
      direction="bottom"
      tooltipText="IBM Corporate Headquarters is based in Armonk, New York."
    >
      Armonk (bottom)
    </TooltipDefinition>
  </Modal>
</Portal>
```

---

### Component API

#### `TooltipDefinition` props

| Prop              | Type                           | Description                                                                                                                                                                               | Default                             |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `open` (Reactive) | `boolean`                      | Set to `true` to open the tooltip.                                                                                                                                                        | `false`                             |
| `ref` (Reactive)  | `null \| HTMLButtonElement`    | Obtain a reference to the button HTML element                                                                                                                                             | `null`                              |
| `tooltipText`     | `string`                       | Specify the tooltip text                                                                                                                                                                  | `""`                                |
| `align`           | `"start" \| "center" \| "end"` | Set the alignment of the tooltip relative to the icon.                                                                                                                                    | `"center"`                          |
| `direction`       | `"top" \| "bottom"`            | Set the direction of the tooltip relative to the icon.                                                                                                                                    | `"bottom"`                          |
| `id`              | `string`                       | Set an id for the tooltip div element                                                                                                                                                     | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `clickToOpen`     | `boolean`                      | By default, the tooltip is opened on hover or focus.<br />Set to `true` to open the tooltip on click/focus instead of on hover.<br />Unhovering or blurring the tooltip will close it.    | `false`                             |
| `enterDelayMs`    | `number`                       | Specify the duration in milliseconds to delay before displaying the tooltip.                                                                                                              | `100`                               |
| `leaveDelayMs`    | `number`                       | Specify the duration in milliseconds to delay before hiding the tooltip.                                                                                                                  | `300`                               |
| `portalTooltip`   | `boolean \| undefined`         | Set to `true` to render the tooltip in a portal,<br />preventing it from being clipped by `overflow: hidden` containers.<br />By default, the tooltip is portalled when inside a `Modal`. | _undefined_                         |

#### `TooltipDefinition` slots

| Slot      | Detail                  |
| --------- | ----------------------- |
| `default` | `Record<string, never>` |
| `tooltip` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `TooltipDefinition` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:focus`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `TooltipDefinition` dispatched events

| Event      | Detail |
| ---------- | ------ |
| `on:close` | `null` |
| `on:open`  | `null` |

#### `TooltipDefinition` $$restProps

`TooltipDefinition` spreads `$$restProps` to the `span` element.


## TooltipIcon

Tooltip icons display contextual information when users hover over or focus on
an icon. They are ideal for providing additional context about icons or actions
in your interface.

### Default

Display a tooltip with the default bottom-center alignment.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon
  tooltipText="Carbon is an open source design system by IBM."
  icon={Carbon}
/>
```

### Reactive example

Control the tooltip state programmatically using `bind:open`.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, Stack, TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";

  let open = true;
</script>

<Stack gap={12}>
  <div>
    <TooltipIcon
      bind:open
      tooltipText="Carbon is an open source design system by IBM."
      icon={Carbon}
      align="start"
    />
  </div>
  <div>
    <Button kind="tertiary" size="small" on:click={() => (open = !open)}>
      {open ? "Close tooltip" : "Open tooltip"}
    </Button>
  </div>
</Stack>
```

### Sizes

Specify the icon size with the `size` prop. Carbon icons use a 16/20/24/32
scale, but any number (pixels) can be used.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon tooltipText="16px" icon={Carbon} size={16} />
<TooltipIcon tooltipText="20px" icon={Carbon} size={20} />
<TooltipIcon tooltipText="24px" icon={Carbon} size={24} />
<TooltipIcon tooltipText="32px" icon={Carbon} size={32} />
```

### Directions

Position the tooltip in different directions and alignments using the
`direction` and `align` props.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Filter from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Filter.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon
  tooltipText="Top start"
  direction="top"
  align="start"
  icon={Filter}
/>
<TooltipIcon
  tooltipText="Right end"
  direction="right"
  align="end"
  icon={Filter}
/>
<TooltipIcon
  tooltipText="Bottom start"
  direction="bottom"
  align="start"
  icon={Filter}
/>
<TooltipIcon
  tooltipText="Left start"
  direction="left"
  align="start"
  icon={Filter}
/>
```

### Custom tooltip text

Use the `tooltipText` slot to customize the tooltip content with additional
styling.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon icon={Carbon}>
  <span slot="tooltipText" style="color: red"
    >Carbon is an open source design system by IBM.</span
  >
</TooltipIcon>
```

### Custom delay

Use `enterDelayMs` and `leaveDelayMs` to customize the delay before showing and
hiding the tooltip on hover. The defaults are 100ms and 300ms, respectively.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon
  tooltipText="Slow to appear"
  icon={Carbon}
  enterDelayMs={500}
  leaveDelayMs={500}
/>
```

### Disabled state

Set `disabled` to `true` to prevent user interaction with the tooltip.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon
  disabled
  tooltipText="Carbon is an open source design system by IBM."
  icon={Carbon}
/>
```

### Portal tooltip

Use `portalTooltip` to render the tooltip in a portal, preventing it from being
clipped by `overflow: hidden` containers. This is automatically enabled when the
tooltip is inside a `Modal`. The `direction` and `align` props are respected
when portalled, matching non-portal positioning.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon
  portalTooltip
  tooltipText="Carbon is an open source design system by IBM."
  icon={Carbon}
/>
```

### Portal (direction)

Icon tooltips support `top`, `right`, `bottom`, and `left`. With
`portalTooltip`, `direction` behaves the same as the default CSS path.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon portalTooltip direction="top" tooltipText="Top" icon={Carbon} />
<TooltipIcon
  portalTooltip
  direction="right"
  tooltipText="Right"
  icon={Carbon}
/>
<TooltipIcon
  portalTooltip
  direction="bottom"
  tooltipText="Bottom"
  icon={Carbon}
/>
<TooltipIcon portalTooltip direction="left" tooltipText="Left" icon={Carbon} />
```

### Portal (alignment)

Portalled tooltips use the same `align` prop as the non-portal path: horizontal
placement along the anchor for `top` and `bottom`, and vertical placement for
`left` and `right`.

```svelte
<script>
  import { TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Carbon from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Carbon.svelte";
</script>

<TooltipIcon
  portalTooltip
  direction="bottom"
  align="start"
  tooltipText="Start"
  icon={Carbon}
/>
<TooltipIcon
  portalTooltip
  direction="bottom"
  align="center"
  tooltipText="Center"
  icon={Carbon}
/>
<TooltipIcon
  portalTooltip
  direction="bottom"
  align="end"
  tooltipText="End"
  icon={Carbon}
/>
```

### Inside a modal

When a tooltip icon is used inside a [Modal](/components/Modal), it is
automatically rendered in a portal so it is not clipped by the modal's overflow
or z-index stacking. Set `portalTooltip` to explicitly control this behavior.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button, Modal, Portal, TooltipIcon } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Filter from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Filter.svelte";

  let open = false;
</script>

<Button on:click={() => (open = true)}>Open modal</Button>

<Portal>
  <Modal
    bind:open
    size="sm"
    modalHeading="TooltipIcon in modal"
    primaryButtonText="Done"
    secondaryButtonText="Cancel"
    on:click:button--secondary={() => (open = false)}
  >
    <TooltipIcon tooltipText="Top" direction="top" icon={Filter} />
    <TooltipIcon tooltipText="Right" direction="right" icon={Filter} />
    <TooltipIcon tooltipText="Bottom" direction="bottom" icon={Filter} />
    <TooltipIcon tooltipText="Left" direction="left" icon={Filter} />
  </Modal>
</Portal>
```

---

### Component API

#### `TooltipIcon` props

| Prop              | Type                                      | Description                                                                                                                                                                               | Default                             |
| ----------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| `open` (Reactive) | `boolean`                                 | Set to `true` to open the tooltip.                                                                                                                                                        | `false`                             |
| `ref` (Reactive)  | `null \| HTMLButtonElement`               | Obtain a reference to the button HTML element                                                                                                                                             | `null`                              |
| `tooltipText`     | `string`                                  | Specify the tooltip text.<br />Alternatively, use the "tooltipText" slot.                                                                                                                 | `""`                                |
| `icon`            | `Icon`                                    | Specify the icon to render.                                                                                                                                                               | _undefined_                         |
| `size`            | `(16 \| 20 \| 24 \| 32 \| (number & {}))` | Specify the icon size.<br />Carbon icons use a 16/20/24/32 scale, but any number (pixels) can be used.                                                                                    | `16`                                |
| `disabled`        | `boolean`                                 | Set to `true` to disable the tooltip icon                                                                                                                                                 | `false`                             |
| `align`           | `"start" \| "center" \| "end"`            | Set the alignment of the tooltip relative to the icon.                                                                                                                                    | `"center"`                          |
| `direction`       | `"top" \| "right" \| "bottom" \| "left"`  | Set the direction of the tooltip relative to the icon.                                                                                                                                    | `"bottom"`                          |
| `id`              | `string`                                  | Set an id for the span element                                                                                                                                                            | `ccs-${Math.random().toString(36)}` |
| `enterDelayMs`    | `number`                                  | Specify the duration in milliseconds to delay before displaying the tooltip.                                                                                                              | `100`                               |
| `leaveDelayMs`    | `number`                                  | Specify the duration in milliseconds to delay before hiding the tooltip.                                                                                                                  | `300`                               |
| `portalTooltip`   | `boolean \| undefined`                    | Set to `true` to render the tooltip in a portal,<br />preventing it from being clipped by `overflow: hidden` containers.<br />By default, the tooltip is portalled when inside a `Modal`. | _undefined_                         |

#### `TooltipIcon` slots

| Slot          | Detail                  |
| ------------- | ----------------------- |
| `default`     | `Record<string, never>` |
| `tooltipText` | `Record<string, never>` |

#### `TooltipIcon` forwarded events

| Event           |
| --------------- |
| `on:blur`       |
| `on:click`      |
| `on:focus`      |
| `on:mouseenter` |
| `on:mouseleave` |
| `on:mouseover`  |

#### `TooltipIcon` dispatched events

| Event      | Detail |
| ---------- | ------ |
| `on:close` | `null` |
| `on:open`  | `null` |

#### `TooltipIcon` $$restProps

`TooltipIcon` spreads `$$restProps` to the `button` element.


## TreeView

A tree view displays hierarchical data in a collapsible tree structure. It
supports node selection, expansion, icons, and programmatic control of the tree
state.

### Default

Create a basic tree view using the `nodes` prop. Each node requires an `id` and
`text`, with optional properties for `disabled`, `icon`, and child `nodes`.

By default only **one** node is selected: `selectedIds` tracks a single id, and
clicking another row replaces the previous selection.

For a multiselectable tree, set `multiselect` to `true` (and optionally
`multiselectMode`); see [Multi-select (node)](#multi-select-node),
[Multi-select (shallow)](#multi-select-shallow), and
[Multi-select (deep)](#multi-select-deep).

> [!NOTE] Every node must have a unique id.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack, TreeView } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let activeId = "";
  let selectedIds = [];
  let nodes = [
    { id: 0, text: "AI / Machine learning" },
    {
      id: 1,
      text: "Analytics",
      nodes: [
        {
          id: 2,
          text: "IBM Analytics Engine",
          nodes: [
            { id: 3, text: "Apache Spark" },
            { id: 4, text: "Hadoop" },
          ],
        },
        { id: 5, text: "IBM Cloud SQL Query" },
        { id: 6, text: "IBM Db2 Warehouse on Cloud" },
      ],
    },
    {
      id: 7,
      text: "Blockchain",
      nodes: [{ id: 8, text: "IBM Blockchain Platform" }],
    },
    {
      id: 9,
      text: "Databases",
      nodes: [
        { id: 10, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for Elasticsearch" },
        { id: 11, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for Enterprise DB" },
        { id: 12, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for MongoDB" },
        { id: 13, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for PostgreSQL" },
      ],
    },
    {
      id: 14,
      text: "Integration",
      disabled: true,
      nodes: [{ id: 15, text: "IBM API Connect", disabled: true }],
    },
  ];
</script>

<Stack gap={6}>
  <div>
    <TreeView
      labelText="Cloud Products"
      {nodes}
      bind:activeId
      bind:selectedIds
      on:select={({ detail }) => console.log("select", detail)}
      on:toggle={({ detail }) => console.log("toggle", detail)}
      on:focus={({ detail }) => console.log("focus", detail)}
    />
  </div>
  <Stack gap={4}>
    <div>Active node id: {activeId}</div>
    <div>Selected ids: {JSON.stringify(selectedIds)}</div>
  </Stack>
</Stack>
```

### Slottable node

Customize node rendering using the default slot with the `let:node` directive.
The node object provides:

| Property     | Description                                   |
| ------------ | --------------------------------------------- |
| **id**       | The node id.                                  |
| **text**     | The node text.                                |
| **expanded** | `true` if the node is expanded.               |
| **leaf**     | `true` if the node does not have child nodes. |
| **disabled** | `true` if the node is disabled.               |
| **selected** | `true` if the node is selected.               |

```svelte
<script>
  import { Stack, TreeView } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let activeId = 0;
  let selectedIds = [0, 7, 9];
  let nodes = [
    { id: 0, text: "AI / Machine learning" },
    {
      id: 1,
      text: "Analytics",
      nodes: [
        {
          id: 2,
          text: "IBM Analytics Engine",
          nodes: [
            { id: 3, text: "Apache Spark" },
            { id: 4, text: "Hadoop" },
          ],
        },
        { id: 5, text: "IBM Cloud SQL Query" },
        { id: 6, text: "IBM Db2 Warehouse on Cloud" },
      ],
    },
    {
      id: 7,
      text: "Blockchain",
      nodes: [{ id: 8, text: "IBM Blockchain Platform" }],
    },
    {
      id: 9,
      text: "Databases",
      nodes: [
        { id: 10, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for Elasticsearch" },
        { id: 11, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for Enterprise DB" },
        { id: 12, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for MongoDB" },
        { id: 13, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for PostgreSQL" },
      ],
    },
    {
      id: 14,
      text: "Integration",
      disabled: true,
      nodes: [{ id: 15, text: "IBM API Connect", disabled: true }],
    },
  ];
</script>

<Stack gap={6}>
  <div>
    <TreeView
      labelText="Cloud Products"
      {activeId}
      {selectedIds}
      {nodes}
      let:node
    >
      <span
        style:color={node.selected ? "var(--cds-interactive-04)" : "inherit"}
        style:text-decoration={node.disabled ? "inherit" : "underline"}
      >
        {node.text}
        (id: {node.id})
      </span>
    </TreeView>
  </div>
</Stack>
```

### Slottable node with checkbox

Slot a checkbox component in each tree view node to create a tree with checkable
items. The checkbox is wrapped in a container with `stopPropagation` to prevent
tree node selection when checking items.

```svelte
<script>
  import { Checkbox, Stack, TreeView } from "carbon-components-svelte";

  let checkedNodes = new Set([0, 7]);
  let nodes = [
    { id: 0, text: "AI / Machine learning" },
    {
      id: 1,
      text: "Analytics",
      nodes: [
        {
          id: 2,
          text: "IBM Analytics Engine",
          nodes: [
            { id: 3, text: "Apache Spark" },
            { id: 4, text: "Hadoop" },
          ],
        },
        { id: 5, text: "IBM Cloud SQL Query" },
        { id: 6, text: "IBM Db2 Warehouse on Cloud" },
      ],
    },
    {
      id: 7,
      text: "Blockchain",
      nodes: [{ id: 8, text: "IBM Blockchain Platform" }],
    },
    {
      id: 9,
      text: "Databases",
      nodes: [
        { id: 10, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for Elasticsearch" },
        { id: 11, text: "IBM Cloud Databases for MongoDB" },
      ],
    },
  ];

  function handleCheck(nodeId) {
    if (checkedNodes.has(nodeId)) {
      checkedNodes.delete(nodeId);
    } else {
      checkedNodes.add(nodeId);
    }
    checkedNodes = checkedNodes;
  }
</script>

<Stack gap={6}>
  <div>
    <TreeView labelText="Cloud Products" {nodes} let:node>
      <div on:click|stopPropagation on:keydown|stopPropagation role="none">
        <Checkbox
          labelText={node.text}
          checked={checkedNodes.has(node.id)}
          disabled={node.disabled}
          on:check={() => handleCheck(node.id)}
        />
      </div>
    </TreeView>
  </div>
  <Stack gap={4}>
    <div>
      <strong>Checked node IDs:</strong>
      {Array.from(checkedNodes)}
    </div>
  </Stack>
</Stack>
```

### Slottable node with inline editing

Use a `contenteditable` element in each tree view node to enable inline editing
of node names. The editable region is wrapped in a container with
`stopPropagation` to prevent tree node selection when editing.

```svelte
<script>
  import { RecursiveList, Stack, TreeView } from "carbon-components-svelte";
  import Edit from "carbon-icons-svelte/lib/Edit.svelte";

  let nodes = [
    { id: 0, text: "AI / Machine learning" },
    {
      id: 1,
      text: "Analytics",
      nodes: [
        {
          id: 2,
          text: "IBM Analytics Engine",
          nodes: [
            { id: 3, text: "Apache Spark" },
            { id: 4, text: "Hadoop" },
          ],
        },
```
